---
title: "`r if (exists('reportTitle')) reportTitle else 'QIIME2 SUSHI Report'`"
output:
  html_document:
    mathjax: https://fgcz-gstore.uzh.ch/reference/mathjax.js
    highlight: pygments
    theme: sand
    code_folding: hide
    toc: yes
    toc_depth: 4
    self_contained: true
    includes:
      in_header: !expr system.file("templates/fgcz_header.html", package="ezRun")
    css: !expr system.file("templates/fgcz.css", package="ezRun")
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: inline
---

<style>
/* fgcz.css sets h1 to 8px (used across FGCZ reports where sections start at
   h2). This report's sections are h1, so restore readable header sizes
   without touching the shared CSS. */
h1 { font-size: 1.9em; font-weight: 700; margin-top: 1.2em; }
h2 { font-size: 1.5em; font-weight: 700; margin-top: 1.0em; }
h3 { font-size: 1.2em; font-weight: 700; }
h4 { font-size: 1.05em; font-weight: 700; }
details > summary { cursor: pointer; }
</style>

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  comment    = '',
  fig.width  = 12,
  fig.height = 8,
  warning    = FALSE,
  error      = FALSE,
  message    = FALSE,
  cache      = TRUE,
  echo       = FALSE
)
```

```{r load_pkgs, include=FALSE}
## Mandatory packages -- the SUSHI app already enforces these in its renv/module
suppressMessages(suppressWarnings({
  library(phyloseq)
  library(qiime2R)
  library(microbiome)
  library(vegan)
  library(pairwiseAdonis)
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(stringr)
  library(tibble)
  library(reshape2)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(ggpubr)
  library(ggrepel)
  library(patchwork)
  library(cowplot)
  library(scales)
  library(RColorBrewer)
  library(rstatix)
  library(kableExtra)
  library(DT)
  library(plotly)
  library(htmlwidgets)
  library(htmltools)
  library(ggvenn)
  library(ComplexHeatmap)
  library(DESeq2)
  library(edgeR)
}))

## Optional packages -- gracefully degrade when not available
.have_ancombc        <- requireNamespace("ANCOMBC",        quietly = TRUE)
.have_aldex2         <- requireNamespace("ALDEx2",         quietly = TRUE)
.have_ggpicrust2     <- requireNamespace("ggpicrust2",     quietly = TRUE)
.have_microbiomeMrk  <- requireNamespace("microbiomeMarker", quietly = TRUE)
.have_dada2          <- requireNamespace("dada2",          quietly = TRUE)
.have_shortread      <- requireNamespace("ShortRead",      quietly = TRUE)
.have_biostrings     <- requireNamespace("Biostrings",     quietly = TRUE)
.have_biomformat     <- requireNamespace("biomformat",     quietly = TRUE)
.have_phangorn       <- requireNamespace("phangorn",       quietly = TRUE)
.have_picante        <- requireNamespace("picante",        quietly = TRUE)
.have_gunifrac       <- requireNamespace("GUniFrac",       quietly = TRUE)
.have_enhancedvolc   <- requireNamespace("EnhancedVolcano",quietly = TRUE)
.have_scatter3d      <- requireNamespace("scatterplot3d",  quietly = TRUE)
.have_microViz       <- requireNamespace("microViz",       quietly = TRUE)
.have_ggtree         <- requireNamespace("ggtree",         quietly = TRUE)
.have_ape            <- requireNamespace("ape",            quietly = TRUE)
.have_metagenomeSeq  <- requireNamespace("metagenomeSeq",  quietly = TRUE)
.have_car            <- requireNamespace("car",            quietly = TRUE)

if (.have_biostrings) suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(library(Biostrings)))
if (.have_shortread) suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(library(ShortRead)))
if (.have_dada2)     suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(library(dada2)))
if (.have_biomformat) suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(library(biomformat)))
if (.have_phangorn)  suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(library(phangorn)))
if (.have_picante)   suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(library(picante)))
if (.have_gunifrac)  suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(library(GUniFrac)))
if (.have_ape)       suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(library(ape)))
if (.have_ggtree)    suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(library(ggtree)))
if (.have_microViz)  suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(library(microViz)))

## shiny::validate masking guard from the project memory
## (jsonlite gets loaded transitively and masks validate)
.qiime2_validate <- function(...) if (requireNamespace("shiny", quietly = TRUE)) shiny::validate(...) else invisible(NULL)
```

```{r load_passed_objects, include=FALSE}
## makeRmdReport() in ezRun (util.R) serialises every named argument it
## receives to <name>.qs2 in the working directory and then renders the Rmd
## in a fresh env. The Rmd must read those objects back in -- nothing is
## auto-bound. Pull in every .qs2 sitting next to the report.
suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(library(qs2)))
.passed_files <- list.files(".", pattern = "\\.qs2$", full.names = FALSE)
for (.f in .passed_files) {
  .nm <- sub("\\.qs2$", "", .f)
  tryCatch(
    assign(.nm, qs_read(.f), envir = knitr::knit_global()),
    error = function(e) message("Failed to qs_read ", .f, ": ", conditionMessage(e))
  )
}
suppressWarnings(rm(list = intersect(c(".passed_files", ".f", ".nm"), ls())))
```

# Configuration

```{r config}
## Pull configuration from the SUSHI `param` object passed by makeRmdReport.
## All knobs are optional: sensible defaults kick in when missing.
if (!exists("param") || is.null(param)) param <- list()

.cfg <- list(
  grouping        = if (!is.null(param$grouping) && nzchar(param$grouping)) param$grouping else "Group",
  group_cols      = if (!is.null(param$grouping) && nzchar(param$grouping)) strsplit(param$grouping, ",")[[1]] else character(0),
  samples_to_prune= if (!is.null(param$samples_to_prune)) param$samples_to_prune else character(0),
  prevalence_frac = if (!is.null(param$prevalence_frac))  param$prevalence_frac  else 0.10,
  rel_abund_pct   = if (!is.null(param$rel_abund_pct))    param$rel_abund_pct    else 0.10,
  forward_primer  = if (!is.null(param$forward_primer))   param$forward_primer   else "CCTACGGGNGGCWGCAG",
  reverse_primer  = if (!is.null(param$reverse_primer))   param$reverse_primer   else "GACTACHVGGGTATCTAATCC",
  run_picrust2    = isTRUE(param$run_picrust2),
  run_fastp       = isTRUE(param$run_fastp)
)

## Resolve output directory. The R backend calls setwdNew(basename(ResultDir))
## and renders the Rmd from inside that working directory, so getwd() is the
## canonical place to drop CSV exports. Do NOT use output$getColumn("ResultDir")
## here -- that returns the gstore destination path that doesn't exist yet at
## render time (SUSHI copies results there only after the job finishes).
.report_dir <- getwd()

## Build a final `grouping` scalar (single column carrying the Group factor inside physeq).
grouping <- "Group"

## Verbose echo for traceability
cat("Configuration:\n")
cat("  Grouping column   :", .cfg$grouping, "\n")
cat("  Group sub-columns :", paste(.cfg$group_cols, collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("  Prevalence filter :", .cfg$prevalence_frac * 100, "% of samples\n")
cat("  Abundance filter  :", .cfg$rel_abund_pct, "% of total reads\n")
cat("  Forward primer    :", .cfg$forward_primer, "\n")
cat("  Reverse primer    :", .cfg$reverse_primer, "\n")
cat("  PICRUSt2 enabled  :", .cfg$run_picrust2, "\n")
cat("  fastp enabled     :", .cfg$run_fastp, "\n")
```

```{r helpers}
## ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
## Helper functions used across the report (defined once, reused everywhere).
## ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Strip square brackets, fix common SILVA quirks (UCG-, A2, group, uncultured).
clean_taxonomy <- function(ps) {
  tax <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ps), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  if ("Genus" %in% colnames(tax)) {
    tax$Genus <- gsub("\\[|\\]", "", tax$Genus)
    tax$Genus[tax$Genus == "uncultured uncultured"] <- "unknown uncultured"
    idx_grp <- grepl("group", tax$Genus)
    tax$Genus[idx_grp] <- str_remove(tax$Genus[idx_grp], "_group")
    tax$Genus[tax$Genus == "UCG-010"] <- "Oscillospirales UCG-010"
    if ("Family" %in% colnames(tax)) {
      idx <- grepl("^UCG", tax$Genus) & !grepl("^UCG", tax$Family) & !is.na(tax$Family)
      tax$Genus[idx] <- paste0(tax$Family[idx], "_", tax$Genus[idx])
    }
    tax$Genus[tax$Genus == "A2"] <- "Lachnospiraceae_A2"
  }
  if ("Family" %in% colnames(tax)) tax$Family <- gsub("\\[|\\]", "", tax$Family)
  tax_table(ps) <- tax_table(as.matrix(tax))
  ps
}

## NULL if no taxa survive after pruning zero-sum taxa.
safe_prune_nonzero <- function(physeq) {
  if (is.null(physeq)) return(NULL)
  keep <- taxa_sums(physeq) > 0
  if (sum(keep) == 0) return(NULL)
  prune_taxa(keep, physeq)
}

## Standard DT widget matching the rest of the FGCZ reports.
fgcz_dt <- function(x, caption = NULL, escape = TRUE, page_length = 10) {
  datatable(x,
            caption    = caption,
            escape     = escape,
            extensions = "Buttons",
            rownames   = FALSE,
            options    = list(scrollX    = TRUE,
                              dom        = "lftBip",
                              buttons    = c("copy", "csv", "excel"),
                              pageLength = page_length,
                              lengthMenu = list(c(10, 20, 100, 500, -1),
                                                c("10", "20", "100", "500", "All"))))
}

## ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
## knit_child machinery for emitting htmlwidgets (DT/plotly) inside dynamically
## generated tabsets. A print(datatable(...)) inside a results='asis' chunk
## emits NOTHING (auto-print via knit_print.htmlwidget only fires for a normal
## chunk's last expression). The reliable way to interleave markdown headers
## with widgets is to build Rmd source text and knit_child() it: the child is
## re-parsed, so headers become sections AND widgets auto-print with their JS
## dependencies. Widgets/plots are stashed in .kc_registry so the generated
## child chunks can reference them by name (avoids recomputation / quoting).
.kc_registry <- new.env(parent = emptyenv())
.kc_counter  <- 0L
## Return Rmd source lines that, when knit, auto-print `obj` in a child chunk.
## Each block gets a UNIQUE label (kc<n>) so multiple knit_child() calls don't
## collide on auto-generated "unnamed-chunk-*" labels, and cache=FALSE so the
## global cache=TRUE doesn't stale these dynamically generated chunks.
kc_block <- function(obj, chunk_opts = "fig.width=8, fig.height=6") {
  .kc_counter <<- .kc_counter + 1L
  nm <- paste0("obj", .kc_counter)
  assign(nm, obj, envir = .kc_registry)
  c(sprintf("```{r kc%d, echo=FALSE, cache=FALSE, %s}", .kc_counter, chunk_opts),
    sprintf(".kc_registry$%s", nm),
    "```", "")
}
## Render a character vector of Rmd source (markdown + kc_block output) as a
## child document and write it straight into the report.
kc_render <- function(lines) {
  cat(knitr::knit_child(text = paste(lines, collapse = "\n"), quiet = TRUE))
  cat("\n\n")
}

## Save a ggplot/plotly widget as a self-contained HTML alongside the report.
save_interactive_widget <- function(widget, fname) {
  fpath <- file.path(.report_dir, fname)
  tryCatch({
    htmlwidgets::saveWidget(widget, fpath, selfcontained = TRUE)
    fname
  }, error = function(e) { message("saveWidget failed: ", e$message); "" })
}

## Aggregate to a rank and return a long df with per-sample rel. abundance.
taxa_aggregate_rank <- function(ps, rank) {
  df <- psmelt(ps)
  df <- df[!is.na(df[[rank]]), , drop = FALSE]
  df %>%
    dplyr::group_by(Sample, Group, !!rlang::sym(rank)) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    dplyr::group_by(Sample) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(SampleTotal  = sum(Abundance, na.rm = TRUE),
                  RelAbundance = ifelse(SampleTotal > 0, Abundance / SampleTotal, 0)) %>%
    dplyr::select(-SampleTotal) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()
}

plot_taxa_bar_all <- function(ps, rank, ylab) {
  agg <- taxa_aggregate_rank(ps, rank)
  if (nrow(agg) == 0) return(ggplot() + theme_void() + ggtitle(paste("No", rank, "data")))
  cols <- colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(12, "Set3"))(length(unique(agg[[rank]])))
  ggplot(agg, aes(x = Sample, y = Abundance, fill = .data[[rank]])) +
    facet_grid(~Group, scales = "free_x") +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "fill") +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1), legend.position = "bottom") +
    labs(y = ylab) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = cols)
}

plot_taxa_bar_topN <- function(ps, rank, N, ylab) {
  agg <- taxa_aggregate_rank(ps, rank)
  if (nrow(agg) == 0) return(ggplot() + theme_void() + ggtitle(paste("No", rank, "data")))
  ranking <- agg %>%
    dplyr::group_by(.data[[rank]]) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(MeanRel = mean(RelAbundance, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(MeanRel))
  top <- as.character(ranking[[rank]][seq_len(min(N, nrow(ranking)))])
  agg$TaxonDisp <- ifelse(as.character(agg[[rank]]) %in% top,
                          as.character(agg[[rank]]), "Other")
  agg <- agg %>%
    dplyr::group_by(Sample, Group, TaxonDisp) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
  lvls <- c(top, "Other")
  lvls <- lvls[lvls %in% unique(agg$TaxonDisp)]
  agg$TaxonDisp <- factor(agg$TaxonDisp, levels = lvls)
  cols <- colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(12, "Set3"))(length(lvls))
  if ("Other" %in% lvls) cols[length(lvls)] <- "grey70"
  ggplot(agg, aes(x = Sample, y = Abundance, fill = TaxonDisp)) +
    facet_grid(~Group, scales = "free_x") +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "fill") +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1), legend.position = "bottom") +
    labs(y = ylab, fill = NULL) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = cols)
}

make_taxa_wide_table <- function(ps, rank) {
  agg <- taxa_aggregate_rank(ps, rank) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(RelAbundancePct = round(RelAbundance * 100, 3)) %>%
    dplyr::select(Sample, !!rlang::sym(rank), RelAbundancePct)
  wide <- tidyr::pivot_wider(agg, names_from = Sample,
                             values_from = RelAbundancePct, values_fill = 0)
  sample_cols <- setdiff(colnames(wide), rank)
  mat <- as.matrix(wide[, sample_cols, drop = FALSE]); mode(mat) <- "numeric"
  wide$`Mean%`       <- round(rowMeans(mat, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
  wide$`Prevalence%` <- round(rowSums(mat > 0, na.rm = TRUE) / length(sample_cols) * 100, 1)
  wide <- wide[, c(rank, "Mean%", "Prevalence%", sample_cols), drop = FALSE]
  wide[order(-wide$`Mean%`), , drop = FALSE]
}

## Per-sample relative abundance transform that doesn't divide by zero.
to_rel <- function(ps) {
  transform_sample_counts(ps, function(x) {
    s <- sum(x, na.rm = TRUE)
    if (s == 0) return(x)
    x / s * 100
  })
}

plot_level_heatmap <- function(ps, level = "Genus", title = "") {
  df <- psmelt(to_rel(ps))
  df <- df[!is.na(df[[level]]), , drop = FALSE]
  if (nrow(df) == 0) return(ggplot() + theme_void() + ggtitle(paste("No", level, "data")))
  df <- df %>%
    dplyr::group_by(Sample, Group, .data[[level]]) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(NormAbundance = log10(Abundance + 0.01))
  ggplot(df, aes(x = Sample, y = .data[[level]], fill = NormAbundance)) +
    geom_tile() +
    facet_grid(~Group, scales = "free_x") +
    scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "Log10 Abundance") +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 8)) +
    labs(y = level, x = "Sample ID", title = title)
}

plot_topN_heatmap <- function(ps, N = 30, title = "") {
  ps_rel <- to_rel(ps)
  taxa_means <- taxa_sums(ps_rel) / nsamples(ps_rel)
  N <- min(N, length(taxa_means))
  topN <- names(sort(taxa_means, decreasing = TRUE)[seq_len(N)])
  df <- psmelt(ps_rel) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      CleanGenus  = dplyr::case_when(
        !is.na(Genus)  & Genus  != "" ~ as.character(Genus),
        !is.na(Family) & Family != "" ~ paste0("f__", Family),
        TRUE ~ as.character(OTU)
      ),
      UniqueTaxa  = paste0(CleanGenus, "_", substr(OTU, 1, 6)),
      DisplayTaxa = dplyr::if_else(OTU %in% topN, UniqueTaxa, "Remainder")
    ) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(Sample, Group, DisplayTaxa) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(NormAbundance = log10(Abundance + 0.01))
  ggplot(df, aes(x = Sample, y = DisplayTaxa, fill = NormAbundance)) +
    geom_tile() +
    facet_grid(~Group, scales = "free_x") +
    scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "Log10 Abundance") +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
    labs(y = paste("Top", N, "Taxa"), x = "Sample ID", title = title)
}

## CLR transform helper: works column-wise (per sample) on a feature×sample matrix.
clr_transform_matrix <- function(mat) {
  mat <- as.matrix(mat) + 0.5  # pseudocount
  apply(mat, 2, function(col) log(col) - mean(log(col)))
}

## Geometric mean for DESeq2 size factor estimation on sparse OTU tables.
gm_mean <- function(x, na.rm = TRUE) exp(sum(log(x[x > 0]), na.rm = na.rm) / length(x))

## Resolve an ANCOM-BC taxon row id to a readable taxonomy.
resolve_taxon_da <- function(ids, tax_tbl) {
  tax_df <- as.data.frame(tax_tbl)
  sapply(ids, function(id) {
    if (id %in% rownames(tax_df)) {
      tx <- tax_df[id, ]
      if (!is.null(tx$Species) && !is.na(tx$Species) && tx$Species != "")
        return(paste0(tx$Genus, " ", tx$Species))
      if (!is.null(tx$Genus)   && !is.na(tx$Genus)   && tx$Genus != "")   return(tx$Genus)
      if (!is.null(tx$Family)  && !is.na(tx$Family)  && tx$Family != "")  return(paste0("f__", tx$Family))
      if (!is.null(tx$Phylum)  && !is.na(tx$Phylum)  && tx$Phylum != "")  return(paste0("p__", tx$Phylum))
    }
    id
  })
}
```

# Data Loading

```{r load_data}
## physeq is supplied by the R backend via makeRmdReport.
## Apply prevalence + relative abundance filters and glom up the standard ranks.

if (!exists("physeq")) stop("`physeq` was not passed to the Rmd by makeRmdReport.")

## Inject combined Group factor if grouping columns are supplied.
if (length(.cfg$group_cols) > 0) {
  sd <- as(sample_data(physeq), "data.frame")
  present <- intersect(.cfg$group_cols, colnames(sd))
  if (length(present) > 0) {
    sd$Group <- do.call(paste, c(lapply(present, function(col) sd[[col]]), sep = "-"))
    sample_data(physeq) <- sample_data(sd)
  } else if (!"Group" %in% colnames(sd)) {
    ## Fall back: pretend everything is one group so the rest of the report still renders.
    sd$Group <- "all"
    sample_data(physeq) <- sample_data(sd)
  }
}

physeq <- clean_taxonomy(physeq)

## Filter 1: prevalence — keep ASVs present (count > 1) in at least N% of samples.
physeq_filter <- genefilter_sample(physeq,
                                   filterfun_sample(function(x) x > 1),
                                   A = .cfg$prevalence_frac * nsamples(physeq))
physeq_prev <- prune_taxa(physeq_filter, physeq)

## Filter 2: relative abundance — drop ASVs below .cfg$rel_abund_pct of total reads.
total_reads <- sum(sample_sums(physeq_prev))
if (total_reads > 0) {
  asv_rel_abund <- taxa_sums(physeq_prev) / total_reads * 100
  physeq_filtered <- prune_taxa(asv_rel_abund >= .cfg$rel_abund_pct, physeq_prev)
} else {
  physeq_filtered <- physeq_prev
}

## Optional manual sample drop list.
if (length(.cfg$samples_to_prune) > 0) {
  drop <- intersect(sample_names(physeq_filtered), .cfg$samples_to_prune)
  if (length(drop) > 0) {
    physeq_filtered <- prune_samples(!sample_names(physeq_filtered) %in% drop, physeq_filtered)
  }
}

## Relative abundance transform of the post-filter object.
physeq_transformed <- to_rel(physeq_filtered)

## Glom to standard ranks (Species optional).
has_species <- "Species" %in% colnames(tax_table(physeq_filtered)) &&
  any(!is.na(tax_table(physeq_filtered)[, "Species"]) &
        tax_table(physeq_filtered)[, "Species"] != "")
physeq_Species <- if (has_species) tax_glom(physeq_filtered, "Species", NArm = TRUE) else NULL
physeq_Genus   <- tax_glom(physeq_filtered, "Genus",   NArm = TRUE)
physeq_Family  <- tax_glom(physeq_filtered, "Family",  NArm = TRUE)
physeq_Phylum  <- tax_glom(physeq_filtered, "Phylum",  NArm = TRUE)

## Build a metadata data.frame to use across the report.
if (!exists("metadata") || is.null(metadata)) {
  metadata <- data.frame(sample_data(physeq_filtered))
  metadata$SampleID <- rownames(metadata)
} else {
  metadata <- as.data.frame(metadata)
  ## Find the sample-id column under any of the common aliases (QIIME2 uses
  ## "sample-id"; R's read.table with default check.names mangles that to
  ## "sample.id"; qiime2R/phyloseq pipelines sometimes pass "SampleID" or "#SampleID").
  if (!"SampleID" %in% colnames(metadata)) {
    .id_aliases <- c("sample-id", "sample.id", "sampleid", "Sample.ID",
                     "Sample-ID", "#SampleID", "sample_id")
    .id_match <- intersect(.id_aliases, colnames(metadata))
    if (length(.id_match) > 0) {
      metadata$SampleID <- as.character(metadata[[.id_match[1]]])
    } else if (!is.null(rownames(metadata)) &&
               !all(rownames(metadata) == as.character(seq_len(nrow(metadata))))) {
      ## Only use rownames if they are NOT the default 1..N integer index.
      metadata$SampleID <- rownames(metadata)
    } else {
      ## Last resort: assume the first column is the sample id.
      metadata$SampleID <- as.character(metadata[[1]])
    }
  }
  if (!"Group" %in% colnames(metadata)) {
    metadata$Group <- as.character(sample_data(physeq_filtered)$Group[match(metadata$SampleID, sample_names(physeq_filtered))])
  }
}

## Levels of the grouping factor — drives all per-subgroup tabsets.
group_levels <- levels(factor(sample_data(physeq_filtered)$Group))
```

# Filtering Summary

```{r filtering_summary}
filt_tbl <- data.frame(
  Stage  = c("Raw input",
             paste0("After ", .cfg$prevalence_frac * 100, "% prevalence filter"),
             paste0("After ", .cfg$rel_abund_pct, "% abundance filter"),
             "Species-level glom",
             "Genus-level glom",
             "Family-level glom",
             "Phylum-level glom"),
  ASVs   = c(ntaxa(physeq), ntaxa(physeq_prev), ntaxa(physeq_filtered),
             if (!is.null(physeq_Species)) ntaxa(physeq_Species) else NA_integer_,
             ntaxa(physeq_Genus), ntaxa(physeq_Family), ntaxa(physeq_Phylum)),
  Samples = c(nsamples(physeq), nsamples(physeq_prev), nsamples(physeq_filtered),
              if (!is.null(physeq_Species)) nsamples(physeq_Species) else NA_integer_,
              nsamples(physeq_Genus), nsamples(physeq_Family), nsamples(physeq_Phylum))
)
kable(filt_tbl, caption = "Filtering pipeline summary") %>% kable_styling()

if (!has_species) {
  cat("\nNote: no Species-level annotations survive filtering -- all Species sub-tabs are skipped.\n")
}
```

# QC {.tabset}

## Library sizes (pre-filter) {.tabset}

### Plot

```{r lib_sizes_pre, fig.width=12, fig.height=8}
## Prefer the pre-computed `library_sizes_pre` object from the R backend
## (single source of truth) and fall back to local recomputation if missing.
.asvs_detected_pre <- colSums(as(otu_table(physeq), "matrix") > 0)[sample_names(physeq)]
if (exists("library_sizes_pre") && !is.null(library_sizes_pre)) {
  .lp <- if (is.data.frame(library_sizes_pre)) {
    if ("SampleID" %in% colnames(library_sizes_pre)) {
      stats::setNames(library_sizes_pre[[setdiff(colnames(library_sizes_pre), "SampleID")[1]]],
                      as.character(library_sizes_pre$SampleID))
    } else stats::setNames(library_sizes_pre[[1]], rownames(library_sizes_pre))
  } else library_sizes_pre
  samp_ids_pre <- intersect(names(.lp), sample_names(physeq))
  if (length(samp_ids_pre) == 0) samp_ids_pre <- sample_names(physeq)
  lib_size_pre_df <- data.frame(
    Sample = samp_ids_pre,
    Reads  = as.numeric(.lp[samp_ids_pre]),
    ASVs_detected = as.integer(.asvs_detected_pre[samp_ids_pre])
  )
} else {
  lib_size_pre_df <- data.frame(
    Sample = sample_names(physeq),
    Reads  = sample_sums(physeq),
    ASVs_detected = .asvs_detected_pre
  )
}
lib_size_pre_df$Sample <- factor(lib_size_pre_df$Sample,
                                 levels = lib_size_pre_df$Sample[order(lib_size_pre_df$Reads)])
p_lib_pre <- ggplot(lib_size_pre_df,
                    aes(x = Reads, y = Sample,
                        text = paste("Sample:", Sample, "<br>Reads:", scales::comma(Reads)))) +
  geom_col(fill = "#00A6A6") +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::comma(Reads)), hjust = -0.1, size = 3) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 10000, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(x = "Read Count", y = "", title = "Library Sizes (before filtering)") +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15)))
ggplotly(p_lib_pre, tooltip = "text")
```

### Table

```{r lib_sizes_pre_table}
lib_pre_out <- data.frame(
  SampleID = as.character(lib_size_pre_df$Sample),
  Reads    = lib_size_pre_df$Reads,
  ASVs_detected = lib_size_pre_df$ASVs_detected
)
write.csv(lib_pre_out, file.path(.report_dir, "library_sizes_pre.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
fgcz_dt(lib_pre_out, "Library sizes (pre-filtering)")
```

## Library sizes (post-filter) {.tabset}

### Plot

```{r lib_sizes_post, fig.width=12, fig.height=8}
## Prefer the pre-computed `library_sizes_post` object from the R backend
## (single source of truth) and fall back to local recomputation if missing.
.asvs_detected_post <- colSums(as(otu_table(physeq_filtered), "matrix") > 0)[sample_names(physeq_filtered)]
if (exists("library_sizes_post") && !is.null(library_sizes_post)) {
  .lpo <- if (is.data.frame(library_sizes_post)) {
    if ("SampleID" %in% colnames(library_sizes_post)) {
      stats::setNames(library_sizes_post[[setdiff(colnames(library_sizes_post), "SampleID")[1]]],
                      as.character(library_sizes_post$SampleID))
    } else stats::setNames(library_sizes_post[[1]], rownames(library_sizes_post))
  } else library_sizes_post
  samp_ids_post <- intersect(names(.lpo), sample_names(physeq_filtered))
  if (length(samp_ids_post) == 0) samp_ids_post <- sample_names(physeq_filtered)
  lib_size_df <- data.frame(
    Sample = samp_ids_post,
    Reads  = as.numeric(.lpo[samp_ids_post]),
    ASVs_detected = as.integer(.asvs_detected_post[samp_ids_post])
  )
} else {
  lib_size_df <- data.frame(
    Sample = sample_names(physeq_filtered),
    Reads  = sample_sums(physeq_filtered),
    ASVs_detected = .asvs_detected_post
  )
}
lib_size_df$Sample <- factor(lib_size_df$Sample,
                             levels = lib_size_df$Sample[order(lib_size_df$Reads)])
p_lib <- ggplot(lib_size_df,
                aes(x = Reads, y = Sample,
                    text = paste("Sample:", Sample, "<br>Reads:", scales::comma(Reads)))) +
  geom_col(fill = "#00A6A6") +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::comma(Reads)), hjust = -0.1, size = 3) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 10000, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(x = "Read Count", y = "", title = "Library Sizes (after filtering)") +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15)))
ggplotly(p_lib, tooltip = "text")
```

### Table

```{r lib_sizes_post_table}
lib_post_out <- data.frame(
  SampleID = as.character(lib_size_df$Sample),
  Reads    = lib_size_df$Reads,
  ASVs_detected = lib_size_df$ASVs_detected
)
write.csv(lib_post_out, file.path(.report_dir, "library_sizes_post.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
fgcz_dt(lib_post_out, "Library sizes (after filtering)")
```

## Read quality profiles

### Forward reads

```{r qc_forward, fig.width=9, fig.height=4.5}
.fnFs <- character(0)
if (exists("raw_reads_path") && length(raw_reads_path) > 0 && nzchar(raw_reads_path[1]) && dir.exists(raw_reads_path[1])) {
  .fnFs <- sort(list.files(raw_reads_path[1],
                           pattern = "_R1\\.fastq\\.gz$|_R1_001\\.fastq\\.gz$",
                           full.names = TRUE))
}
if (length(.fnFs) > 0 && .have_dada2) {
  qc_F <- dada2::plotQualityProfile(.fnFs, aggregate = TRUE) +
    ggtitle("Forward Reads -- Aggregated Quality Profile")
  print(qc_F)
} else {
  cat("Forward-read FASTQs not available -- skipping quality profile.\n")
}
```

### Reverse reads

```{r qc_reverse, fig.width=9, fig.height=4.5}
.fnRs <- character(0)
if (exists("raw_reads_path") && length(raw_reads_path) > 0 && nzchar(raw_reads_path[1]) && dir.exists(raw_reads_path[1])) {
  .fnRs <- sort(list.files(raw_reads_path[1],
                           pattern = "_R2\\.fastq\\.gz$|_R2_001\\.fastq\\.gz$",
                           full.names = TRUE))
}
if (length(.fnRs) > 0 && .have_dada2) {
  qc_R <- dada2::plotQualityProfile(.fnRs, aggregate = TRUE) +
    ggtitle("Reverse Reads -- Aggregated Quality Profile")
  print(qc_R)
} else {
  cat("Reverse-read FASTQs not available -- skipping quality profile.\n")
}
```

## Primer presence check

```{r primer_check}
.primer_check_df <- NULL
if (exists("primer_check") && !is.null(primer_check) && is.data.frame(primer_check)) {
  .primer_check_df <- primer_check
} else if (exists("dada2_rep_seqs_fasta") &&
           length(dada2_rep_seqs_fasta) > 0 && nzchar(dada2_rep_seqs_fasta[1]) &&
           file.exists(dada2_rep_seqs_fasta[1]) && .have_shortread) {
  seqs <- ShortRead::sread(ShortRead::readFasta(dada2_rep_seqs_fasta[1]))
  n_seqs <- length(seqs)
  fwd_hits <- Biostrings::vcountPattern(.cfg$forward_primer, seqs, fixed = FALSE)
  rev_hits <- Biostrings::vcountPattern(.cfg$reverse_primer, seqs, fixed = FALSE)
  rev_comp_r <- as.character(Biostrings::reverseComplement(Biostrings::DNAString(.cfg$reverse_primer)))
  rev_comp_hits <- Biostrings::vcountPattern(rev_comp_r, seqs, fixed = FALSE)
  .primer_check_df <- data.frame(
    Primer        = c("Forward (5')", "Reverse (3')", "Reverse complement"),
    Sequence      = c(.cfg$forward_primer, .cfg$reverse_primer, rev_comp_r),
    Seqs_with_hit = c(sum(fwd_hits > 0), sum(rev_hits > 0), sum(rev_comp_hits > 0)),
    Percent       = c(round(sum(fwd_hits > 0) / n_seqs * 100, 2),
                      round(sum(rev_hits > 0) / n_seqs * 100, 2),
                      round(sum(rev_comp_hits > 0) / n_seqs * 100, 2))
  )
}

if (!is.null(.primer_check_df)) {
  kable(.primer_check_df, caption = "Primer presence in representative sequences") %>% kable_styling()
  if (any(.primer_check_df$Percent > 5, na.rm = TRUE)) {
    cat("\nWARNING: primers detected in >5% of sequences -- consider revising trim parameters.\n")
  } else if (all(.primer_check_df$Percent < 1, na.rm = TRUE)) {
    cat("\nPrimers successfully removed (<1% residual).\n")
  }
} else {
  cat("No representative-sequence FASTA available -- skipping primer check.\n")
}
```

## DADA2 denoise stats

```{r dada_denoise_table}
.denoise_df <- NULL
if (exists("denoise_stats_raw") && !is.null(denoise_stats_raw)) {
  .denoise_df <- as.data.frame(denoise_stats_raw)
} else if (exists("dada2") && !is.null(dada2)) {
  .denoise_df <- tryCatch({
    if (is.list(dada2) && !is.null(dada2$data)) as.data.frame(dada2$data) else as.data.frame(dada2)
  }, error = function(e) NULL)
}
if (!is.null(.denoise_df)) {
  if (!"SampleID" %in% colnames(.denoise_df)) {
    .denoise_df <- tibble::rownames_to_column(.denoise_df, "SampleID")
  }
  write.csv(.denoise_df, file.path(.report_dir, "denoise_stats.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  fgcz_dt(.denoise_df, "DADA2 denoising stats per sample")
} else {
  cat("DADA2 denoise stats not available.\n")
}
```

```{r dada_denoise_plot, fig.width=12, fig.height=6}
if (!is.null(.denoise_df) && "non-chimeric" %in% colnames(.denoise_df) ||
    !is.null(.denoise_df) && "non.chimeric" %in% colnames(.denoise_df) ||
    !is.null(.denoise_df) && "non_chimeric" %in% colnames(.denoise_df)) {
  ncol_name <- intersect(c("non-chimeric", "non.chimeric", "non_chimeric"), colnames(.denoise_df))[1]
  d2 <- .denoise_df
  d2$.nonchim <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(as.character(d2[[ncol_name]])))
  d2 <- d2[!is.na(d2$.nonchim), , drop = FALSE]
  if (nrow(d2) > 0) {
    p_reads <- ggplot(d2, aes(x = SampleID, y = .nonchim, fill = SampleID)) +
      geom_col() +
      theme_bw() +
      geom_text(aes(label = scales::comma(.nonchim)), vjust = -0.3, size = 3) +
      labs(title = "Reads retained after DADA2", x = NULL, y = "Non-chimeric reads") +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), legend.position = "none")
    print(p_reads)
  }
}
```

## fastp QC

```{r fastp_qc_build, results='asis'}
## Split into a text (asis) chunk + a widget chunk: DT widgets are only emitted
## into the report when auto-printed from a NORMAL chunk (see asv_table_blast
## note). Here we build the data frames and emit only prose; the next chunk
## auto-prints the tables via an htmltools::tagList.
## `fastp_reports` may be a character vector / list of paths or a data.frame.
.fastp_ok <- exists("fastp_reports") && !is.null(fastp_reports) && length(fastp_reports) > 0
.fastp_summary   <- NULL
.fastp_links_df  <- NULL
.fastp_json_df   <- NULL
if (.fastp_ok) {
  .fastp_paths <- if (is.data.frame(fastp_reports)) {
    path_col <- intersect(c("path", "Path", "report", "Report", "html", "HTML",
                            "json", "JSON", "file", "File"),
                          colnames(fastp_reports))
    if (length(path_col) > 0) as.character(fastp_reports[[path_col[1]]])
    else as.character(fastp_reports[[ncol(fastp_reports)]])
  } else {
    as.character(unlist(fastp_reports))
  }
  .fastp_paths <- .fastp_paths[nzchar(.fastp_paths)]

  ## Split HTML vs JSON reports.
  .fastp_html <- .fastp_paths[grepl("\\.html?$", .fastp_paths, ignore.case = TRUE)]
  .fastp_json <- .fastp_paths[grepl("\\.json$",  .fastp_paths, ignore.case = TRUE)]

  ## Best-effort sample naming -- strip the common fastp suffix patterns.
  derive_sample <- function(p) {
    bn <- basename(p)
    bn <- sub("\\.fastp\\.(html|json)$", "", bn, ignore.case = TRUE)
    bn <- sub("_fastp\\.(html|json)$",  "", bn, ignore.case = TRUE)
    bn <- sub("\\.(html|json)$",         "", bn, ignore.case = TRUE)
    bn
  }

  ## Try to summarise reads before/after from JSON, defensively. Paths that do
  ## not resolve (e.g. relative paths after a setwd) are skipped, so we resolve
  ## against the report dir as a fallback.
  .resolve_fastp <- function(p) {
    if (file.exists(p)) return(p)
    alt <- file.path(.report_dir, p)
    if (file.exists(alt)) return(alt)
    alt2 <- file.path(.report_dir, basename(dirname(p)), basename(p))
    if (file.exists(alt2)) return(alt2)
    p
  }
  if (length(.fastp_json) > 0 && requireNamespace("jsonlite", quietly = TRUE)) {
    rows <- lapply(.fastp_json, function(jp) {
      js <- tryCatch(jsonlite::fromJSON(.resolve_fastp(jp), simplifyVector = TRUE),
                     error = function(e) NULL)
      if (is.null(js)) return(NULL)
      rb <- tryCatch(js$summary$before_filtering$total_reads, error = function(e) NA)
      ra <- tryCatch(js$summary$after_filtering$total_reads,  error = function(e) NA)
      if (is.null(rb)) rb <- NA
      if (is.null(ra)) ra <- NA
      data.frame(Sample      = derive_sample(jp),
                 Reads_before = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(rb)),
                 Reads_after  = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(ra)),
                 stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    })
    rows <- rows[!sapply(rows, is.null)]
    if (length(rows) > 0) {
      .fastp_summary <- do.call(rbind, rows)
      .fastp_summary$Pct_kept <- ifelse(
        is.na(.fastp_summary$Reads_before) | .fastp_summary$Reads_before == 0, NA,
        round(.fastp_summary$Reads_after / .fastp_summary$Reads_before * 100, 2)
      )
    }
  }

  if (!is.null(.fastp_summary) && nrow(.fastp_summary) > 0) {
    cat("Per-sample reads before and after fastp filtering:\n\n")
    write.csv(.fastp_summary,
              file.path(.report_dir, "fastp_summary.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  } else {
    cat("Per-sample fastp JSON summaries unavailable (JSON shape unfamiliar or jsonlite missing). ",
        "Showing report links only.\n\n", sep = "")
  }

  if (length(.fastp_html) > 0) {
    .fastp_links_df <- data.frame(
      Sample = derive_sample(.fastp_html),
      Report = sprintf('<a href="%s" target="_blank" rel="noopener">view</a>', .fastp_html),
      Path   = .fastp_html,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  } else if (length(.fastp_json) > 0) {
    .fastp_json_df <- data.frame(Sample = derive_sample(.fastp_json),
                                 JSON   = .fastp_json,
                                 stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  }
} else {
  cat("fastp not run (run_fastp = false) or no reports available -- skipping fastp QC.\n")
}
```

```{r fastp_qc_tables}
## Auto-printed htmlwidgets (normal chunk). tagList emits every widget it holds
## together with their JS/CSS dependencies.
.fastp_widgets <- list()
if (!is.null(.fastp_summary) && nrow(.fastp_summary) > 0) {
  .fastp_widgets[[length(.fastp_widgets) + 1]] <- fgcz_dt(.fastp_summary, "fastp summary (per sample)")
}
if (!is.null(.fastp_links_df)) {
  .fastp_widgets[[length(.fastp_widgets) + 1]] <- DT::datatable(
    .fastp_links_df, caption = "fastp HTML reports", escape = FALSE, rownames = FALSE,
    extensions = "Buttons",
    options = list(scrollX = TRUE, dom = "lftBip", buttons = c("copy", "csv", "excel"),
                   pageLength = 10, lengthMenu = c(10, 20, 100, 200)))
} else if (!is.null(.fastp_json_df)) {
  .fastp_widgets[[length(.fastp_widgets) + 1]] <- fgcz_dt(.fastp_json_df, "fastp JSON reports")
}
if (length(.fastp_widgets) > 0) htmltools::tagList(.fastp_widgets) else invisible(NULL)
```

# ASV table with BLAST links

```{r asv_table_blast}
## NOTE: this is a NORMAL chunk (no results='asis'). An htmlwidget (DT) is only
## emitted into the report when knitr auto-prints it via knit_print.htmlwidget,
## which happens for the LAST top-level expression of a normal chunk. Under
## results='asis' a print(datatable(...)) silently produces nothing. So we
## build `asv_tax_df` here and leave the datatable as the final expression.
## Fast-path: if the R backend already passed a fully-built `asv_taxonomy_df`
## (with ASV_ID + per-sample counts + BLAST link), use it directly.
.use_passed_asv_df <- exists("asv_taxonomy_df") && !is.null(asv_taxonomy_df) &&
  is.data.frame(asv_taxonomy_df) &&
  any(c("ASV_ID", "Feature.ID", "Feature ID", "FeatureID") %in% colnames(asv_taxonomy_df))

## physeq_all is needed for downstream chunks too -- compute once here.
physeq_all <- if (exists("physeq_unfiltered") && !is.null(physeq_unfiltered)) {
  clean_taxonomy(physeq_unfiltered)
} else if (exists("table_unfiltered") && !is.null(table_unfiltered)) {
  clean_taxonomy(table_unfiltered)
} else {
  physeq
}

if (.use_passed_asv_df &&
    "ASV_ID" %in% colnames(asv_taxonomy_df) &&
    "BLAST"  %in% colnames(asv_taxonomy_df) &&
    any(sample_names(physeq) %in% colnames(asv_taxonomy_df))) {
  ## Backend provided a complete, render-ready table.
  asv_tax_df <- as.data.frame(asv_taxonomy_df, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  write.csv(asv_tax_df[, setdiff(colnames(asv_tax_df), "BLAST"), drop = FALSE],
            file.path(.report_dir, "asv_ids_taxonomy.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  .asv_caption <- paste0("All ASVs (", nrow(asv_tax_df), " total).")
} else {
  asv_tax_df <- data.frame(
    ASV_ID = taxa_names(physeq_all),
    as.data.frame(tax_table(physeq_all)),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  ## Merge in any extra columns from the backend-provided asv_taxonomy_df
  ## (e.g. the QIIME2 raw Taxon string / Confidence) so users can still see
  ## the source taxonomy alongside the cleaned phyloseq tax_table.
  if (.use_passed_asv_df) {
    extra <- as.data.frame(asv_taxonomy_df, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    id_col <- intersect(c("ASV_ID", "Feature.ID", "Feature ID", "FeatureID"),
                        colnames(extra))[1]
    if (!is.null(id_col) && id_col != "ASV_ID") {
      extra$ASV_ID <- as.character(extra[[id_col]])
    }
    keep_cols <- setdiff(colnames(extra),
                         c(colnames(asv_tax_df), id_col))
    if (length(keep_cols) > 0) {
      extra <- extra[, c("ASV_ID", keep_cols), drop = FALSE]
      asv_tax_df <- dplyr::left_join(asv_tax_df, extra, by = "ASV_ID")
    }
  }

  otu_pre_mat <- as(otu_table(physeq_all), "matrix")
  if (!taxa_are_rows(physeq_all)) otu_pre_mat <- t(otu_pre_mat)
  otu_pre_mat <- otu_pre_mat[asv_tax_df$ASV_ID, , drop = FALSE]
  sample_cols <- colnames(otu_pre_mat)
  asv_tax_df$Total_reads <- as.integer(rowSums(otu_pre_mat))
  asv_tax_df$N_samples   <- as.integer(rowSums(otu_pre_mat > 0))
  asv_tax_df$In_filtered <- asv_tax_df$ASV_ID %in% taxa_names(physeq_filtered)

  ## Attach representative sequences + BLAST link if available.
  .rep_seqs <- NULL
  if (exists("rep_seqs") && !is.null(rep_seqs)) {
    .rep_seqs <- if (inherits(rep_seqs, "DNAStringSet")) rep_seqs
                 else if (is.list(rep_seqs) && !is.null(rep_seqs$data)) rep_seqs$data
                 else NULL
  } else if (exists("dada2_rep_seqs_fasta") &&
             length(dada2_rep_seqs_fasta) > 0 && file.exists(dada2_rep_seqs_fasta[1]) &&
             .have_biostrings) {
    .rep_seqs <- tryCatch(Biostrings::readDNAStringSet(dada2_rep_seqs_fasta[1]),
                          error = function(e) NULL)
  }
  if (!is.null(.rep_seqs) && length(.rep_seqs) > 0) {
    seq_lookup <- setNames(as.character(.rep_seqs), names(.rep_seqs))
    asv_tax_df$Sequence <- seq_lookup[asv_tax_df$ASV_ID]
    asv_tax_df$Length   <- nchar(asv_tax_df$Sequence)
    asv_tax_df$BLAST    <- ifelse(
      is.na(asv_tax_df$Sequence), "",
      sprintf('<a href="https://blast.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/Blast.cgi?PROGRAM=blastn&PAGE_TYPE=BlastSearch&DATABASE=nt&QUERY=%s" target="_blank" rel="noopener">BLAST</a>',
              asv_tax_df$Sequence)
    )
  }

  ## Per-sample read counts at the end so BLAST stays visible.
  asv_tax_df[, sample_cols] <- as.data.frame(otu_pre_mat, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

  ## CSV without the HTML BLAST column.
  write.csv(asv_tax_df[, setdiff(colnames(asv_tax_df), "BLAST")],
            file.path(.report_dir, "asv_ids_taxonomy.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

  .asv_caption <- paste0("All ASVs (", nrow(asv_tax_df),
                         " total). In_filtered=TRUE marks the ",
                         sum(asv_tax_df$In_filtered),
                         " ASVs that survived the prevalence + abundance filter.")
}

## Final expression: auto-printed as an htmlwidget (do NOT wrap in print()).
DT::datatable(asv_tax_df,
  caption = .asv_caption,
  escape  = FALSE,
  extensions = "Buttons", rownames = FALSE,
  options = list(scrollX = TRUE, dom = "lftBip",
                 buttons = c("copy", "csv", "excel"),
                 pageLength = 10, lengthMenu = c(10, 20, 100, 200)))
```

# Taxa ASVs abundance per sample {.tabset}

## Pre-filtering

```{r prevalence_pre, fig.width=15, fig.height=7}
otu_full <- as(otu_table(physeq_all), "matrix")
if (!taxa_are_rows(physeq_all)) otu_full <- t(otu_full)
asv_per_sample <- colSums(otu_full)
asv_df <- data.frame(
  SampleID      = names(asv_per_sample),
  Total_reads   = asv_per_sample,
  ASVs_detected = colSums(otu_full > 0)
)
p_pre <- ggplot(asv_df, aes(x = SampleID, y = Total_reads, fill = SampleID)) +
  geom_col() +
  theme_bw() +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::comma(Total_reads)), vjust = -0.3, size = 3) +
  labs(title = "Total ASV abundance per sample (pre-filter)",
       x = NULL, y = "Total ASV abundance") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), legend.position = "none")
print(p_pre)
write.csv(asv_df, file.path(.report_dir, "asv_per_sample_pre.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
fgcz_dt(asv_df, "Per-sample read totals and ASV richness (pre-filter)")
```

## After filtering

```{r prevalence_post, fig.width=15, fig.height=7}
otu_filt <- as(otu_table(physeq_filtered), "matrix")
if (!taxa_are_rows(physeq_filtered)) otu_filt <- t(otu_filt)
asv_df_filt <- data.frame(
  SampleID      = colnames(otu_filt),
  Total_reads   = colSums(otu_filt),
  ASVs_detected = colSums(otu_filt > 0)
)
p_post <- ggplot(asv_df_filt, aes(x = SampleID, y = Total_reads, fill = SampleID)) +
  geom_col() +
  theme_bw() +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::comma(Total_reads)), vjust = -0.3, size = 3) +
  labs(title = "Total ASV abundance per sample (after filtering)",
       x = NULL, y = "Total ASV abundance") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), legend.position = "none")
print(p_post)
write.csv(asv_df_filt, file.path(.report_dir, "asv_per_sample_post.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
fgcz_dt(asv_df_filt, "Per-sample read totals and ASV richness (after filter)")
```

# Taxa prevalence {.tabset}

Taxa prevalence is how widespread a taxon is across samples (independent of abundance).
The All samples tabset shows the global view; per-group sub-tabsets are generated
automatically for every level of the grouping factor.

```{r prevalence_helpers}
## Drop rows where the target rank is missing/blank/prefix-only.
## SILVA-cleaned tax_tables often carry `""`, `"NA"`, or bare `"g__"` strings,
## none of which is.na() would catch — we filter all of those.
remove_na_rank <- function(ps, rank) {
  tax <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ps), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  vals <- as.character(tax[[rank]])
  keep <- !is.na(vals) & nzchar(vals) & vals != "NA" &
          !grepl("^[a-z]__$", vals)
  if (sum(keep) == 0) return(NULL)
  prune_taxa(rownames(tax)[keep], ps)
}

## Custom prevalence plot: agglomerate to `rank` (dropping taxa unclassified at
## that rank), then plot prevalence (fraction of samples in which the taxon
## occurs) vs total abundance, coloured by Phylum. Returns NULL only when no
## taxon is classified at the rank (e.g. Species with empty s__ everywhere) --
## replaces microbiome::plot_taxa_prevalence, which drew sparse/empty panels.
custom_prevalence_plot <- function(ps, rank, title) {
  ps_r <- tryCatch(tax_glom(ps, rank, NArm = TRUE), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(ps_r) || ntaxa(ps_r) == 0) return(NULL)
  otu <- as(otu_table(ps_r), "matrix")
  if (!taxa_are_rows(ps_r)) otu <- t(otu)
  nsamp <- ncol(otu)
  if (nsamp == 0) return(NULL)
  tax <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ps_r), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  df <- data.frame(
    Taxon          = tax[[rank]],
    Phylum         = if ("Phylum" %in% colnames(tax)) tax[["Phylum"]] else NA_character_,
    Prevalence     = rowSums(otu > 0) / nsamp,
    TotalAbundance = rowSums(otu),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  df <- df[df$TotalAbundance > 0, , drop = FALSE]
  if (nrow(df) == 0) return(NULL)
  ## `text` is not a real ggplot aesthetic, but plotly::ggplotly(tooltip="text")
  ## reads it to build the hover label -- taxon name, prevalence, abundance.
  df$text <- paste0(rank, ": ", df$Taxon,
                    "<br>Prevalence: ", round(df$Prevalence, 3),
                    " (", round(df$Prevalence * nsamp), "/", nsamp, " samples)",
                    "<br>Total abundance: ", df$TotalAbundance,
                    "<br>Phylum: ", df$Phylum)
  ggplot(df, aes(x = TotalAbundance, y = Prevalence, color = Phylum, text = text)) +
    geom_point(size = 2.5, alpha = 0.75) +
    scale_x_log10() +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +
    labs(x = "Total abundance (reads, log10)",
         y = "Prevalence (fraction of samples)", title = title) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
}

## Build Rmd source lines for one prevalence panel: an interactive plotly widget
## (hover shows taxon/prevalence/abundance) wrapped in kc_block so it renders
## inside a dynamically generated tabset, or an italic note when there is no
## data / no taxa classified at the rank. Returns a character vector to splice
## into a knit_child() document.
prev_lines <- function(ps, rank, title) {
  if (is.null(ps)) return(paste0("_", title, " -- no data_\n"))
  p <- tryCatch(custom_prevalence_plot(ps, rank, title), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(p)) return(paste0("_", title, " -- no taxa classified at ", rank, "._\n"))
  w <- tryCatch(
    plotly::ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text") %>% plotly::layout(showlegend = FALSE),
    error = function(e) NULL)
  ## Fall back to the static ggplot if ggplotly ever fails, so a panel is never blank.
  kc_block(if (is.null(w)) p else w, chunk_opts = "fig.width=12, fig.height=6")
}
```

```{r prev_all_dynamic, results='asis'}
## Every prevalence panel is an interactive plotly widget (hover a point to read
## its taxon, prevalence and total abundance). plotly/DT widgets do NOT auto-print
## under results='asis', so we build the whole section as Rmd source (markdown
## headers + kc_block widget chunks) and knit_child it -- the same pattern used by
## the CLR and PICRUSt2 sections. Ranks ordered Species -> Genus -> Family -> Phylum.
.prev_ranks <- c("Species", "Genus", "Family", "Phylum")

.prev_all_lines <- function(ps, stage) {
  unlist(lapply(.prev_ranks, function(rk) {
    if (rk == "Species" && !has_species) return(character(0))
    c(sprintf("### %s", rk), "",
      prev_lines(ps, rk, sprintf("%s -- All samples (%s)", rk, stage)), "")
  }))
}

kc_render(c("## Pre-filtering (all samples) {.tabset}", "",
            .prev_all_lines(physeq, "pre")))
kc_render(c("## After filtering (all samples) {.tabset}", "",
            .prev_all_lines(physeq_filtered, "post")))

## Per-group tabsets (post-filter)
.prev_grp_lines <- c("## Per-group tabsets (post-filter) {.tabset}", "")
for (grp in group_levels) {
  .keep <- as.character(sample_data(physeq_filtered)$Group) == grp
  ps_grp <- safe_prune_nonzero(prune_samples(.keep, physeq_filtered))
  if (is.null(ps_grp)) {
    .prev_grp_lines <- c(.prev_grp_lines, sprintf("### %s", grp), "", "_No data in this group._", "")
    next
  }
  .prev_grp_lines <- c(.prev_grp_lines, sprintf("### %s {.tabset}", grp), "")
  for (rk in .prev_ranks) {
    if (rk == "Species" && !has_species) next
    .prev_grp_lines <- c(.prev_grp_lines, sprintf("#### %s", rk), "",
                         prev_lines(ps_grp, rk, sprintf("%s -- %s", rk, grp)), "")
  }
}
kc_render(.prev_grp_lines)
```

# Heatmaps {.tabset}

Log10-transformed relative abundance heatmaps. Each value is `log10(rel% + 0.01)`; absent taxa render at `-2`.

## Species level (post-filter)
```{r heatmap_species_post, fig.width=20, fig.height=12, eval=has_species}
plot_level_heatmap(physeq_filtered, level = "Species", title = "Species -- Post-filter")
```

## Genus level (pre-filter)
```{r heatmap_genus_pre, fig.width=20, fig.height=12}
plot_level_heatmap(physeq, level = "Genus", title = "Genus -- Pre-filter")
```

## Genus level (post-filter)
```{r heatmap_genus_post, fig.width=20, fig.height=12}
plot_level_heatmap(physeq_filtered, level = "Genus", title = "Genus -- Post-filter")
```

## Family level (post-filter)
```{r heatmap_family_post, fig.width=20, fig.height=12}
plot_level_heatmap(physeq_filtered, level = "Family", title = "Family -- Post-filter")
```

## Phylum level (post-filter)
```{r heatmap_phylum_post, fig.width=20, fig.height=12}
plot_level_heatmap(physeq_filtered, level = "Phylum", title = "Phylum -- Post-filter")
```

## Top 30 ASVs (post-filter)
```{r heatmap_top30_post, fig.width=20, fig.height=14}
plot_topN_heatmap(physeq_filtered, N = 30, title = "Top 30 ASVs -- Post-filter")
```

## Interactive heatmaps (HTML files)

```{r heatmap_interactive, results='asis'}
items <- list(
  list("Genus -- Pre",  function() plot_level_heatmap(physeq, "Genus", "Genus -- Pre"),  "interactive_heatmap_genus_pre.html",  TRUE),
  list("Genus -- Post", function() plot_level_heatmap(physeq_filtered, "Genus", "Genus -- Post"), "interactive_heatmap_genus_post.html", TRUE),
  list("Family -- Post",function() plot_level_heatmap(physeq_filtered, "Family", "Family -- Post"), "interactive_heatmap_family_post.html", TRUE),
  list("Phylum -- Post",function() plot_level_heatmap(physeq_filtered, "Phylum", "Phylum -- Post"), "interactive_heatmap_phylum_post.html", TRUE),
  list("Top 30 -- Post",function() plot_topN_heatmap(physeq_filtered, 30, "Top 30 ASVs -- Post"), "interactive_heatmap_top30_post.html", TRUE),
  list("Species -- Post",function() plot_level_heatmap(physeq_filtered, "Species", "Species -- Post"), "interactive_heatmap_species_post.html", has_species)
)
for (it in items) {
  if (!it[[4]]) next
  tryCatch({
    widget <- ggplotly(it[[2]]())
    fn <- save_interactive_widget(widget, it[[3]])
    if (nzchar(fn)) cat(sprintf("- [%s](%s)\n", it[[1]], fn))
  }, error = function(e) cat(sprintf("- %s skipped: %s\n", it[[1]], e$message)))
}
```

# Composition barplots {.tabset}

Relative abundance per sample, faceted by Group. The All tab shows every taxon at the rank;
Top 20 / Top 50 lump the rest into "Other".

```{r barplots_setup}
.do_bar_rank <- function(rank) {
  cat(sprintf("\n### %s {.tabset}\n\n", rank))
  cat("#### All\n\n")
  print(plot_taxa_bar_all(physeq, rank, paste("Relative Abundance", rank, "(pre)")))
  print(plot_taxa_bar_all(physeq_filtered, rank, paste("Relative Abundance", rank, "(post)")))
  cat("\n\n#### Top 20\n\n")
  print(plot_taxa_bar_topN(physeq, rank, 20, paste("Top 20", rank, "(pre)")))
  print(plot_taxa_bar_topN(physeq_filtered, rank, 20, paste("Top 20", rank, "(post)")))
  cat("\n\n#### Top 50\n\n")
  print(plot_taxa_bar_topN(physeq, rank, 50, paste("Top 50", rank, "(pre)")))
  print(plot_taxa_bar_topN(physeq_filtered, rank, 50, paste("Top 50", rank, "(post)")))
}
```

## Species
```{r barplot_species, fig.width=20, fig.height=12, eval=has_species}
print(plot_taxa_bar_all(physeq_filtered, "Species", "Relative Abundance Species (post)"))
print(plot_taxa_bar_topN(physeq_filtered, "Species", 20, "Top 20 Species (post)"))
print(plot_taxa_bar_topN(physeq_filtered, "Species", 50, "Top 50 Species (post)"))
species_tbl_post <- make_taxa_wide_table(physeq_filtered, "Species")
write.csv(species_tbl_post, file.path(.report_dir, "taxa_abundance_species_post.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
fgcz_dt(species_tbl_post, "Species -- Relative abundance (%) per sample, post-filter")
```

## Genus
```{r barplot_genus, fig.width=20, fig.height=12}
print(plot_taxa_bar_all(physeq_filtered, "Genus", "Relative Abundance Genus (post)"))
print(plot_taxa_bar_topN(physeq_filtered, "Genus", 20, "Top 20 Genus (post)"))
print(plot_taxa_bar_topN(physeq_filtered, "Genus", 50, "Top 50 Genus (post)"))
genus_tbl_post <- make_taxa_wide_table(physeq_filtered, "Genus")
write.csv(genus_tbl_post, file.path(.report_dir, "taxa_abundance_genus_post.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
fgcz_dt(genus_tbl_post, "Genus -- Relative abundance (%) per sample, post-filter")
```

## Family
```{r barplot_family, fig.width=20, fig.height=12}
print(plot_taxa_bar_all(physeq_filtered, "Family", "Relative Abundance Family (post)"))
print(plot_taxa_bar_topN(physeq_filtered, "Family", 20, "Top 20 Family (post)"))
print(plot_taxa_bar_topN(physeq_filtered, "Family", 50, "Top 50 Family (post)"))
family_tbl_post <- make_taxa_wide_table(physeq_filtered, "Family")
write.csv(family_tbl_post, file.path(.report_dir, "taxa_abundance_family_post.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
fgcz_dt(family_tbl_post, "Family -- Relative abundance (%) per sample, post-filter")
```

## Phylum
```{r barplot_phylum, fig.width=20, fig.height=12}
print(plot_taxa_bar_all(physeq_filtered, "Phylum", "Relative Abundance Phylum (post)"))
print(plot_taxa_bar_topN(physeq_filtered, "Phylum", 20, "Top 20 Phylum (post)"))
phylum_tbl_post <- make_taxa_wide_table(physeq_filtered, "Phylum")
write.csv(phylum_tbl_post, file.path(.report_dir, "taxa_abundance_phylum_post.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
fgcz_dt(phylum_tbl_post, "Phylum -- Relative abundance (%) per sample, post-filter")
```

## Interactive (HTML files)

```{r barplot_interactive, results='asis'}
ranks_iv <- c("Phylum", "Family", "Genus")
if (has_species) ranks_iv <- c("Species", ranks_iv)
for (rank in ranks_iv) {
  for (state in c("pre", "post")) {
    ps_use <- if (state == "pre") physeq else physeq_filtered
    tryCatch({
      p <- plot_taxa_bar_all(ps_use, rank, paste("Relative Abundance", rank, "(", state, ")"))
      widget <- ggplotly(p)
      fn <- save_interactive_widget(widget,
                                    paste0("interactive_barplot_", tolower(rank), "_", state, ".html"))
      if (nzchar(fn)) cat(sprintf("- [%s -- %s-filter](%s)\n", rank, state, fn))
    }, error = function(e) cat(sprintf("- %s %s skipped: %s\n", rank, state, e$message)))
  }
}
```

# Phylogenetic tree

```{r phylo_tree, fig.width=15, fig.height=10}
.tree_obj <- NULL
if (exists("tree") && !is.null(tree)) {
  .tree_obj <- tree
} else if (!is.null(phy_tree(physeq_filtered, errorIfNULL = FALSE))) {
  .tree_obj <- phy_tree(physeq_filtered)
}

if (!is.null(.tree_obj)) {
  if (.have_ggtree) {
    tryCatch({
      melt_simple <- psmelt(physeq_filtered) %>%
        dplyr::filter(Abundance < 50) %>%
        dplyr::select(OTU, val = Abundance)
      p <- ggtree::ggtree(physeq_filtered, layout = "fan", open.angle = 10) +
        ggtree::geom_tippoint(mapping = aes(color = Genus), size = 1.5, show.legend = TRUE)
      print(p + ggtitle("Phylogenetic tree (ggtree)"))
    }, error = function(e) {
      cat("ggtree plot failed: ", e$message, "\n")
      if (.have_ape) ape::plot.phylo(.tree_obj, type = "fan", show.tip.label = FALSE,
                                     main = "Phylogenetic tree (ape fallback)")
    })
  } else if (.have_ape) {
    ape::plot.phylo(.tree_obj, type = "fan", show.tip.label = FALSE,
                    main = "Phylogenetic tree")
  } else {
    cat("Neither ggtree nor ape available -- skipping phylogenetic tree.\n")
  }
} else {
  cat("No tree object available -- skipping phylogenetic tree.\n")
}
```

# Venn diagram across grouping levels

```{r venn, fig.width=20, fig.height=9}
otu_pre  <- as(otu_table(physeq), "matrix")
if (!taxa_are_rows(physeq)) otu_pre <- t(otu_pre)
otu_post <- as(otu_table(physeq_filtered), "matrix")
if (!taxa_are_rows(physeq_filtered)) otu_post <- t(otu_post)

groups_post <- as.character(sample_data(physeq_filtered)$Group)
names(groups_post) <- sample_names(physeq_filtered)

make_group_sets <- function(otu_mat, samp_groups) {
  setNames(lapply(unique(samp_groups), function(g) {
    samp_in <- names(samp_groups)[samp_groups == g]
    samp_in <- intersect(samp_in, colnames(otu_mat))
    if (length(samp_in) == 0) return(character(0))
    asvs <- rowSums(otu_mat[, samp_in, drop = FALSE] >= 1) > 0
    rownames(otu_mat)[asvs]
  }), unique(samp_groups))
}

sets_post <- make_group_sets(otu_post, groups_post)

if (length(sets_post) >= 2 && length(sets_post) <= 5) {
  print(ggvenn(sets_post, set_name_size = 4, text_size = 4) +
          ggtitle(paste("Shared ASVs across", length(sets_post), "groups")))
} else if (length(sets_post) > 5) {
  m <- make_comb_mat(rapply(sets_post, na.omit, how = "replace"), mode = "distinct")
  print(UpSet(m,
              comb_order = order(comb_size(m), decreasing = TRUE),
              top_annotation = upset_top_annotation(m, add_numbers = TRUE),
              right_annotation = upset_right_annotation(m, add_numbers = TRUE)))
}

## Membership table
membership <- as.data.frame(
  do.call(cbind, lapply(sets_post, function(asvs) as.integer(rownames(otu_post) %in% asvs))),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
membership$ASV_ID    <- rownames(otu_post)
membership$N_groups  <- rowSums(membership[, names(sets_post), drop = FALSE])
write.csv(membership, file.path(.report_dir, "venn_membership_post.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
fgcz_dt(head(membership[order(-membership$N_groups), ], 200),
        "Top 200 ASVs by group-membership count (post-filter)")
```

# Alpha diversity {.tabset}

Shannon -- richness + evenness combined. Observed -- raw ASV count. Faith's PD -- phylogenetic
breadth (summed branch lengths). Rarefaction equalises sequencing depth across samples.

## Rarefaction curve

```{r rarecurve, fig.width=6, fig.height=6}
otu_tab <- t(as(otu_table(physeq_filtered), "matrix"))
if (taxa_are_rows(physeq_filtered)) otu_tab <- as(otu_table(physeq_filtered), "matrix") %>% t()
tryCatch(rarecurve(otu_tab, step = 50, cex = 0.5),
         error = function(e) cat("rarecurve failed: ", e$message, "\n"))
```

## All measures (rarefied)

```{r alpha_div_rarefied, fig.width=14, fig.height=10}
set.seed(123)
ps_clean <- prune_samples(sample_sums(physeq_filtered) > 0, physeq_filtered)
ps_clean <- prune_taxa(taxa_sums(ps_clean) > 0, ps_clean)
min_lib  <- min(sample_sums(ps_clean))

ps_rarefied <- tryCatch(rarefy_even_depth(ps_clean, sample.size = min_lib, rngseed = 123,
                                          replace = FALSE, verbose = FALSE),
                        error = function(e) NULL)

richness_rar <- NULL
if (!is.null(ps_rarefied)) {
  richness_rar <- estimate_richness(ps_rarefied,
                                    measures = c("Observed", "Chao1", "Shannon", "Simpson")) %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column("SampleID")
  richness_rar$SampleID <- str_remove(richness_rar$SampleID, "^X")

  ## Prefer QIIME2-supplied evenness vector (Pielou's) from `evenness$data`
  ## over re-computing with microbiome::evenness.
  pielou_rar <- NULL
  if (exists("evenness") && !is.null(evenness)) {
    .ev_df <- if (is.list(evenness) && !is.null(evenness$data)) evenness$data else evenness
    .ev_df <- as.data.frame(.ev_df, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    if (!"SampleID" %in% colnames(.ev_df)) {
      .ev_df <- tibble::rownames_to_column(.ev_df, "SampleID")
    }
    ## QIIME2 column is usually `pielou_evenness`; rename to `pielou` to match
    ## downstream alpha_metrics intersect().
    ev_val_col <- setdiff(colnames(.ev_df), "SampleID")[1]
    if (!is.null(ev_val_col) && ev_val_col != "pielou") {
      names(.ev_df)[names(.ev_df) == ev_val_col] <- "pielou"
    }
    pielou_rar <- .ev_df[, c("SampleID", "pielou"), drop = FALSE]
  } else {
    pielou_rar <- microbiome::evenness(ps_rarefied, index = "pielou") %>%
      tibble::rownames_to_column("SampleID")
  }

  ## Prefer QIIME2-supplied observed-features vector from `richness$data` over
  ## the locally-estimated Observed column (overwrite when available).
  if (exists("richness") && !is.null(richness)) {
    .rich_df <- if (is.list(richness) && !is.null(richness$data)) richness$data else richness
    .rich_df <- as.data.frame(.rich_df, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    if (!"SampleID" %in% colnames(.rich_df)) {
      .rich_df <- tibble::rownames_to_column(.rich_df, "SampleID")
    }
    rich_val_col <- setdiff(colnames(.rich_df), "SampleID")[1]
    if (!is.null(rich_val_col)) {
      names(.rich_df)[names(.rich_df) == rich_val_col] <- "Observed.qiime2"
      richness_rar <- dplyr::left_join(richness_rar, .rich_df[, c("SampleID", "Observed.qiime2")],
                                       by = "SampleID")
      ## Replace Observed with QIIME2's value where available.
      ok <- !is.na(richness_rar$Observed.qiime2)
      richness_rar$Observed[ok] <- richness_rar$Observed.qiime2[ok]
      richness_rar$Observed.qiime2 <- NULL
    }
  }

  meta_rar   <- as(sample_data(ps_rarefied), "data.frame") %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column("SampleID")
  richness_rar <- richness_rar %>%
    dplyr::left_join(pielou_rar, by = "SampleID") %>%
    dplyr::left_join(meta_rar,   by = "SampleID")
  richness_rar$Shannon.Effective <- round(exp(richness_rar$Shannon), 2)

  ## Prefer QIIME2-supplied Faith's PD vector over the picante recomputation.
  if (exists("faith_pd") && !is.null(faith_pd)) {
    .fp_df <- if (is.list(faith_pd) && !is.null(faith_pd$data)) faith_pd$data else faith_pd
    .fp_df <- as.data.frame(.fp_df, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    if (!"SampleID" %in% colnames(.fp_df)) {
      .fp_df <- tibble::rownames_to_column(.fp_df, "SampleID")
    }
    fp_val_col <- setdiff(colnames(.fp_df), "SampleID")[1]
    if (!is.null(fp_val_col) && fp_val_col != "Faiths.PD") {
      names(.fp_df)[names(.fp_df) == fp_val_col] <- "Faiths.PD"
    }
    richness_rar <- dplyr::left_join(richness_rar,
                                     .fp_df[, c("SampleID", "Faiths.PD")],
                                     by = "SampleID")
  } else if (.have_phangorn && !is.null(phy_tree(ps_rarefied, errorIfNULL = FALSE)) && .have_picante) {
    my_tree <- phy_tree(ps_rarefied)
    if (!ape::is.rooted(my_tree)) my_tree <- phangorn::midpoint(my_tree)
    faith_rar <- tryCatch(picante::pd(t(as.data.frame(otu_table(ps_rarefied))),
                                      my_tree, include.root = TRUE),
                          error = function(e) NULL)
    if (!is.null(faith_rar)) {
      faith_rar$SampleID <- rownames(faith_rar)
      richness_rar <- richness_rar %>%
        dplyr::left_join(faith_rar[, c("SampleID", "PD")], by = "SampleID") %>%
        dplyr::rename(Faiths.PD = PD)
    }
  }

  lev <- levels(as.factor(richness_rar$Group))
  L.pairs <- if (length(lev) >= 2) combn(seq_along(lev), 2, simplify = FALSE, FUN = function(i) lev[i]) else list()
  alpha_metrics <- intersect(c("Observed", "Chao1", "Shannon", "Simpson", "pielou", "Shannon.Effective", "Faiths.PD"),
                             colnames(richness_rar))
  alpha_plots <- lapply(alpha_metrics, function(metric) {
    p <- ggviolin(richness_rar, x = "Group", y = metric, fill = "Group",
                  add = "boxplot", add.params = list(fill = "white", outlier.shape = NA)) +
      theme_bw() + ggtitle(paste(metric, "-- Rarefied")) +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
    if (length(L.pairs) > 0) {
      p <- p + stat_compare_means(comparisons = L.pairs, method = "wilcox.test", label = "p.format")
    }
    p
  })
  if (length(alpha_plots) > 0) print(wrap_plots(alpha_plots, ncol = 3))
} else {
  cat("Rarefaction failed -- skipping rarefied alpha diversity.\n")
}
```

## Shannon Kruskal-Wallis

```{r kw_test}
.use_alpha <- NULL
if (!is.null(richness_rar) && "Shannon" %in% colnames(richness_rar)) {
  .use_alpha <- richness_rar
} else if (exists("shannon") && !is.null(shannon)) {
  s <- if (is.list(shannon) && !is.null(shannon$data)) shannon$data else as.data.frame(shannon)
  .use_alpha <- s %>% tibble::rownames_to_column("SampleID")
  if (!"Shannon" %in% colnames(.use_alpha) && "shannon_entropy" %in% colnames(.use_alpha)) {
    .use_alpha$Shannon <- .use_alpha$shannon_entropy
  }
  .use_alpha <- dplyr::left_join(.use_alpha, metadata, by = "SampleID")
}

if (!is.null(.use_alpha) && "Shannon" %in% colnames(.use_alpha) && "Group" %in% colnames(.use_alpha)) {
  if (length(unique(.use_alpha$Group)) >= 2) {
    kw <- kruskal.test(Shannon ~ as.factor(Group), data = .use_alpha)
    cat("Global Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = ", round(kw$statistic, 3), "\n")
    cat("Global Kruskal-Wallis p-value     = ", signif(kw$p.value, 4), "\n\n")

    pw <- tryCatch(pairwise_wilcox_test(data = .use_alpha,
                                        formula = Shannon ~ Group,
                                        p.adjust.method = "BH"),
                   error = function(e) NULL)
    if (!is.null(pw)) {
      write.csv(pw, file.path(.report_dir, "alpha_diversity_pairwise_wilcoxon.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
      fgcz_dt(pw[, intersect(c("group1", "group2", "n1", "n2", "statistic", "p", "p.adj", "p.adj.signif"),
                              colnames(pw))],
              "Pairwise Wilcoxon (Shannon)")
    }
  } else {
    cat("Only one group level present -- skipping KW test.\n")
  }
} else {
  cat("Shannon values not available -- skipping KW test.\n")
}
```

## Faith's PD

```{r faith_pd_tab, fig.width=8, fig.height=6}
## Render a Faith's PD violin/boxplot by Group with Kruskal-Wallis, using the
## QIIME2-supplied `faith_pd` vector. Guarded so the section disappears when
## the input is missing (e.g. older runs without faith_pd_vector.qza).
if (exists("faith_pd") && !is.null(faith_pd)) {
  .fp_df_tab <- if (is.list(faith_pd) && !is.null(faith_pd$data)) faith_pd$data else faith_pd
  .fp_df_tab <- as.data.frame(.fp_df_tab, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  if (!"SampleID" %in% colnames(.fp_df_tab)) {
    .fp_df_tab <- tibble::rownames_to_column(.fp_df_tab, "SampleID")
  }
  fp_val_col <- setdiff(colnames(.fp_df_tab), "SampleID")[1]
  if (!is.null(fp_val_col) && fp_val_col != "Faiths.PD") {
    names(.fp_df_tab)[names(.fp_df_tab) == fp_val_col] <- "Faiths.PD"
  }
  .fp_df_tab <- dplyr::left_join(.fp_df_tab, metadata[, intersect(c("SampleID", "Group"),
                                                                 colnames(metadata)), drop = FALSE],
                                 by = "SampleID")
  .fp_df_tab <- .fp_df_tab[!is.na(.fp_df_tab$Faiths.PD) & !is.na(.fp_df_tab$Group), , drop = FALSE]

  if (nrow(.fp_df_tab) > 0 && length(unique(.fp_df_tab$Group)) >= 2) {
    write.csv(.fp_df_tab, file.path(.report_dir, "alpha_diversity_faith_pd.csv"),
              row.names = FALSE)
    lev_fp <- levels(as.factor(.fp_df_tab$Group))
    L.pairs.fp <- combn(seq_along(lev_fp), 2, simplify = FALSE,
                        FUN = function(i) lev_fp[i])
    p_fp <- ggviolin(.fp_df_tab, x = "Group", y = "Faiths.PD", fill = "Group",
                     add = "boxplot",
                     add.params = list(fill = "white", outlier.shape = NA)) +
      theme_bw() + ggtitle("Faith's PD by Group") +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
      ylab("Faith's PD")
    if (length(L.pairs.fp) > 0) {
      p_fp <- p_fp + stat_compare_means(comparisons = L.pairs.fp,
                                        method = "wilcox.test",
                                        label = "p.format")
    }
    print(p_fp)

    kw_fp <- kruskal.test(Faiths.PD ~ as.factor(Group), data = .fp_df_tab)
    cat("\nKruskal-Wallis (Faith's PD) chi-squared =", round(kw_fp$statistic, 3), "\n")
    cat("Kruskal-Wallis (Faith's PD) p-value     =", signif(kw_fp$p.value, 4), "\n")

    pw_fp <- tryCatch(pairwise_wilcox_test(data = .fp_df_tab,
                                           formula = Faiths.PD ~ Group,
                                           p.adjust.method = "BH"),
                      error = function(e) NULL)
    if (!is.null(pw_fp)) {
      write.csv(pw_fp,
                file.path(.report_dir, "alpha_diversity_faith_pd_pairwise_wilcoxon.csv"),
                row.names = FALSE)
      fgcz_dt(pw_fp[, intersect(c("group1", "group2", "n1", "n2", "statistic",
                                  "p", "p.adj", "p.adj.signif"), colnames(pw_fp))],
              "Pairwise Wilcoxon (Faith's PD)")
    }
  } else {
    cat("Faith's PD vector available but not enough groups -- skipping comparison.\n")
  }
} else {
  cat("Faith's PD vector not provided -- skipping (older runs without faith_pd_vector.qza).\n")
}
```

# Beta diversity {.tabset}

PERMANOVA R^2 = fraction of community variation explained by Group. Betadisper checks
homogeneity of group dispersions; significant betadisper means PERMANOVA differences may
partly reflect dispersion rather than location.

## Jaccard {.tabset}

```{r beta_jaccard_prep}
.jaccard_data <- NULL
.jaccard_mat  <- NULL
if (exists("jaccard") && !is.null(jaccard)) {
  .jaccard_data <- if (is.list(jaccard) && !is.null(jaccard$data)) jaccard$data else jaccard
}
if (exists("jaccardmatrix") && !is.null(jaccardmatrix)) {
  .jaccard_mat <- if (is.list(jaccardmatrix) && !is.null(jaccardmatrix$data)) as.matrix(jaccardmatrix$data) else as.matrix(jaccardmatrix)
}
if (is.null(.jaccard_mat)) {
  .jaccard_mat <- tryCatch(as.matrix(phyloseq::distance(physeq_filtered, method = "jaccard")),
                           error = function(e) NULL)
}
```

### PCoA

```{r jaccard_pcoa, fig.width=7, fig.height=6}
if (!is.null(.jaccard_mat)) {
  ord_j <- ordinate(physeq_filtered, "PCoA", as.dist(.jaccard_mat))
  print(plot_ordination(physeq_filtered, ord_j, color = "Group") +
          geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.6) + stat_ellipse() + theme_bw() +
          ggtitle("Jaccard PCoA"))
} else {
  cat("No Jaccard matrix available.\n")
}
```

### PERMANOVA

```{r jaccard_permanova}
if (!is.null(.jaccard_mat)) {
  meta2 <- metadata[metadata$SampleID %in% rownames(.jaccard_mat), , drop = FALSE]
  .ng <- length(unique(na.omit(meta2$Group)))
  if (.ng < 2) {
    cat("PERMANOVA skipped: needs >=2 Group levels (found ", .ng, ").\n", sep = "")
  } else {
    perm  <- vegan::adonis2(.jaccard_mat ~ meta2$Group, permutations = 999, method = "jaccard")
    cat("Global PERMANOVA p-value =", signif(perm$`Pr(>F)`[1], 3), "\n")
    cat("Global PERMANOVA F        =", round(perm$F[1], 2), "\n")
    if (.ng >= 3) {
      pw_jac <- pairwise.adonis(.jaccard_mat, meta2$Group, perm = 999, sim.method = "jaccard")
      fgcz_dt(pw_jac, "Pairwise PERMANOVA -- Jaccard")
    }
  }
}
```

### Betadispersion

```{r jaccard_betadisper}
if (!is.null(.jaccard_mat)) {
  meta2 <- metadata[metadata$SampleID %in% rownames(.jaccard_mat), , drop = FALSE]
  .ng <- length(unique(na.omit(meta2$Group)))
  if (.ng < 2) {
    cat("Betadispersion skipped: needs >=2 Group levels (found ", .ng, ").\n", sep = "")
  } else {
    bd <- vegan::betadisper(as.dist(.jaccard_mat), meta2$Group)
    print(anova(bd))
    print(vegan::permutest(bd, pairwise = TRUE))
  }
}
```

## Bray-Curtis {.tabset}

```{r beta_bray_prep}
.bray_data <- NULL
.bray_mat  <- NULL
if (exists("bray") && !is.null(bray)) {
  .bray_data <- if (is.list(bray) && !is.null(bray$data)) bray$data else bray
}
if (exists("braymatrix") && !is.null(braymatrix)) {
  .bray_mat <- if (is.list(braymatrix) && !is.null(braymatrix$data)) as.matrix(braymatrix$data) else as.matrix(braymatrix)
}
if (is.null(.bray_mat)) {
  .bray_mat <- tryCatch(as.matrix(phyloseq::distance(physeq_filtered, method = "bray")),
                        error = function(e) NULL)
}
```

### PCoA

```{r bray_pcoa, fig.width=7, fig.height=6}
if (!is.null(.bray_mat)) {
  ord_b <- ordinate(physeq_filtered, "PCoA", as.dist(.bray_mat))
  print(plot_ordination(physeq_filtered, ord_b, color = "Group") +
          geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.6) + stat_ellipse() + theme_bw() +
          ggtitle("Bray-Curtis PCoA"))
}
```

### NMDS

```{r bray_nmds, fig.width=7, fig.height=6}
if (!is.null(.bray_mat)) {
  tryCatch({
    ps_rel <- microbiome::transform(physeq_filtered, "compositional")
    p <- plot_landscape(ps_rel, "NMDS", "bray", col = grouping) +
           labs(title = "NMDS / Bray")
    print(p)
  }, error = function(e) cat("NMDS failed: ", e$message, "\n"))
}
```

### PERMANOVA

```{r bray_permanova}
if (!is.null(.bray_mat)) {
  meta2 <- metadata[metadata$SampleID %in% rownames(.bray_mat), , drop = FALSE]
  .ng <- length(unique(na.omit(meta2$Group)))
  if (.ng < 2) {
    cat("PERMANOVA skipped: needs >=2 Group levels (found ", .ng, ").\n", sep = "")
  } else {
    perm  <- vegan::adonis2(.bray_mat ~ meta2$Group, permutations = 999, method = "bray")
    cat("Global PERMANOVA p-value =", signif(perm$`Pr(>F)`[1], 3), "\n")
    cat("Global PERMANOVA F        =", round(perm$F[1], 2), "\n")
    if (.ng >= 3) {
      pw_bc <- pairwise.adonis(.bray_mat, meta2$Group, perm = 999, sim.method = "bray")
      fgcz_dt(pw_bc, "Pairwise PERMANOVA -- Bray-Curtis")
    }
  }
}
```

### Betadispersion

```{r bray_betadisper}
if (!is.null(.bray_mat)) {
  meta2 <- metadata[metadata$SampleID %in% rownames(.bray_mat), , drop = FALSE]
  .ng <- length(unique(na.omit(meta2$Group)))
  if (.ng < 2) {
    cat("Betadispersion skipped: needs >=2 Group levels (found ", .ng, ").\n", sep = "")
  } else {
    bd <- vegan::betadisper(as.dist(.bray_mat), meta2$Group)
    print(anova(bd))
    print(vegan::permutest(bd, pairwise = TRUE))
  }
}
```

## Weighted UniFrac {.tabset}

```{r beta_unifrac_prep}
.uf_mat <- NULL
if (exists("unifracmatrix") && !is.null(unifracmatrix)) {
  .uf_mat <- if (is.list(unifracmatrix) && !is.null(unifracmatrix$data)) as.matrix(unifracmatrix$data) else as.matrix(unifracmatrix)
}
if (is.null(.uf_mat) && !is.null(phy_tree(physeq_filtered, errorIfNULL = FALSE))) {
  .uf_mat <- tryCatch(as.matrix(phyloseq::distance(physeq_filtered, method = "wunifrac")),
                      error = function(e) NULL)
}

## Prefer the QIIME2-computed PCoA vectors from `unifrac$data$Vectors` for the
## scatter plot (matches the Jaccard/Bray pcoa_results pattern). Falls back to
## the local ordinate() call if the full unifrac QZA was not supplied.
.uf_vectors <- NULL
if (exists("unifrac") && !is.null(unifrac)) {
  .uf_vectors <- tryCatch({
    v <- if (is.list(unifrac) && !is.null(unifrac$data) &&
             !is.null(unifrac$data$Vectors)) unifrac$data$Vectors else NULL
    if (!is.null(v)) as.data.frame(v, stringsAsFactors = FALSE) else NULL
  }, error = function(e) NULL)
}
```

### PCoA

```{r uf_pcoa, fig.width=7, fig.height=6}
if (!is.null(.uf_vectors) && nrow(.uf_vectors) > 0) {
  ## QIIME2 pcoa_results -> Vectors: SampleID + Axis.1 + Axis.2 + ...
  uf_v <- .uf_vectors
  id_col <- intersect(c("SampleID", "Sample.ID", "Sample-ID", "sample-id",
                        "SampleId", "sample_id"), colnames(uf_v))
  if (length(id_col) == 0) {
    ## Some qiime2R versions put the id in the first column; coerce to SampleID.
    uf_v$SampleID <- as.character(uf_v[[1]])
    id_col <- "SampleID"
  } else {
    names(uf_v)[names(uf_v) == id_col[1]] <- "SampleID"
    id_col <- "SampleID"
  }
  ax_cols <- grep("^(PC|Axis\\.?)\\s*\\d+$", colnames(uf_v), value = TRUE)
  if (length(ax_cols) < 2) ax_cols <- setdiff(colnames(uf_v), "SampleID")[1:2]
  pc1 <- ax_cols[1]; pc2 <- ax_cols[2]
  uf_v <- dplyr::left_join(uf_v, metadata[, intersect(c("SampleID", "Group"),
                                                     colnames(metadata)), drop = FALSE],
                           by = "SampleID")
  print(ggplot(uf_v, aes(x = .data[[pc1]], y = .data[[pc2]], color = Group)) +
          geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.6) +
          stat_ellipse() +
          theme_bw() +
          labs(x = pc1, y = pc2, title = "Weighted UniFrac PCoA (QIIME2)"))
} else if (!is.null(.uf_mat)) {
  ord_u <- ordinate(physeq_filtered, "PCoA", as.dist(.uf_mat))
  print(plot_ordination(physeq_filtered, ord_u, color = "Group") +
          geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.6) + stat_ellipse() + theme_bw() +
          ggtitle("Weighted UniFrac PCoA"))
}
```

### PERMANOVA

```{r uf_permanova}
if (!is.null(.uf_mat)) {
  meta2 <- metadata[metadata$SampleID %in% rownames(.uf_mat), , drop = FALSE]
  .ng <- length(unique(na.omit(meta2$Group)))
  if (.ng < 2) {
    cat("PERMANOVA skipped: needs >=2 Group levels (found ", .ng, ").\n", sep = "")
  } else {
    perm  <- vegan::adonis2(.uf_mat ~ meta2$Group, permutations = 999)
    cat("Global PERMANOVA p-value =", signif(perm$`Pr(>F)`[1], 3), "\n")
    cat("Global PERMANOVA F        =", round(perm$F[1], 2), "\n")
    if (.ng >= 3) {
      pw_uf <- pairwise.adonis(.uf_mat, meta2$Group, perm = 999)
      fgcz_dt(pw_uf, "Pairwise PERMANOVA -- Weighted UniFrac")
    }
  }
}
```

### Betadispersion

```{r uf_betadisper}
if (!is.null(.uf_mat)) {
  meta2 <- metadata[metadata$SampleID %in% rownames(.uf_mat), , drop = FALSE]
  .ng <- length(unique(na.omit(meta2$Group)))
  if (.ng < 2) {
    cat("Betadispersion skipped: needs >=2 Group levels (found ", .ng, ").\n", sep = "")
  } else {
    bd <- vegan::betadisper(as.dist(.uf_mat), meta2$Group)
    print(anova(bd))
    print(vegan::permutest(bd, pairwise = TRUE))
  }
}
```

# Differential abundance {.tabset}

## Properties of taxonomic profiling data

```{r da_props, fig.width=12, fig.height=4}
otu_mat_f <- as(otu_table(physeq_filtered), "matrix")
if (!taxa_are_rows(physeq_filtered)) otu_mat_f <- t(otu_mat_f)

## Overdispersion: variance > mean
df_disp <- reshape2::melt(otu_mat_f, varnames = c("Taxon", "Sample"), value.name = "Reads") %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Taxon) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(mean = mean(Reads), variance = var(Reads), .groups = "drop")
p_disp <- ggplot(df_disp, aes(x = mean, y = variance)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) + geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1) +
  scale_x_log10() + scale_y_log10() +
  labs(title = "Overdispersion (variance > mean)") + theme_bw()

## Sparsity
log10ab <- reshape2::melt(log10(1 + otu_mat_f))$value
p_sparse <- ggplot(data.frame(v = log10ab), aes(x = v)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 80) + theme_bw() +
  labs(title = "Sparsity (log10 abundance distribution)", x = "log10(1 + reads)")

## Rarity
df_rare <- reshape2::melt(otu_mat_f) %>% dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(value))
