```{r da_props, fig.width=12, fig.height=4}
df_rare$Var1 <- factor(df_rare$Var1, levels = unique(df_rare$Var1))
p_rare <- ggplot(df_rare, aes(x = Var1, y = value)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.5) + theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_blank()) +
  labs(title = "Rarity (per-taxon abundance)",
       x = "Taxonomic group", y = "Reads") +
  scale_y_log10()

print(p_disp + p_sparse + p_rare + plot_layout(ncol = 3))

cat("\n**Rarefy or not?**\n\n",
    "We do *not* rarefy the count table for differential abundance because rarefaction throws away data ",
    "and introduces artificial uncertainty. See ",
    "[Waste Not, Want Not](https://journals.plos.org/ploscompbiol/article?id=10.1371/journal.pcbi.1003531) ",
    "for the canonical argument.\n")
```

## ANCOM-BC2 {.tabset}

ANCOM-BC2 (`ANCOMBC::ancombc2`) fits a log-linear model with bias correction and reports
per-taxon log-fold-changes, adjusted p-values, and a structural-zero flag for taxa absent
in entire groups. It is run at Species (when available), Genus and Family. With more than two
groups it also reports the Global test, all Pairwise contrasts, and Dunnett-type contrasts
against the reference level.

```{r ancombc2_run, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
## Robust ANCOM-BC2 runner. On sparse multi-group data (e.g. distinct body sites)
## every taxon can be flagged as a structural zero, which makes ancombc2 abort with
## "All taxa contain structural zeros". We use the conservative structural-zero
## definition (neg_lb = FALSE) and, if the call still fails, transparently retry with
## struc_zero = FALSE so the section is never empty. lib_cut is kept at 0 because this
## workflow supports shallow libraries -- a non-zero lib_cut could drop every sample.
.ancombc2_msg <- list()
.ancombc2_run <- function(tax_level) {
  if (!.have_ancombc || length(group_levels) < 2) return(NULL)
  ps_ok <- switch(tax_level,
                  Species = !is.null(physeq_Species) && ntaxa(physeq_Species) > 0,
                  Genus   = ntaxa(physeq_Genus)  > 0,
                  Family  = ntaxa(physeq_Family) > 0,
                  FALSE)
  if (!isTRUE(ps_ok)) return(NULL)
  multi <- length(group_levels) > 2
  base_args <- list(
    data = physeq_filtered, fix_formula = "Group", tax_level = tax_level,
    p_adj_method = "BH", lib_cut = 0, prv_cut = .cfg$prevalence_frac,
    group = "Group", neg_lb = FALSE, alpha = 0.05, pseudo_sens = FALSE,
    global = multi, pairwise = multi, dunnet = multi,
    iter_control = list(tol = 1e-2, max_iter = 20, verbose = FALSE),
    em_control   = list(tol = 1e-5, max_iter = 100))
  res <- tryCatch(do.call(ANCOMBC::ancombc2, c(base_args, list(struc_zero = TRUE))),
                  error = function(e) { .ancombc2_msg[[tax_level]] <<- e$message; NULL })
  if (is.null(res)) {
    res <- tryCatch(do.call(ANCOMBC::ancombc2, c(base_args, list(struc_zero = FALSE))),
                    error = function(e) { .ancombc2_msg[[tax_level]] <<- e$message; NULL })
    if (!is.null(res)) attr(res, "sz_fallback") <- TRUE
  }
  res
}
.ancombc2_res <- list(
  Species = if (has_species) .ancombc2_run("Species") else NULL,
  Genus   = .ancombc2_run("Genus"),
  Family  = .ancombc2_run("Family"))
```

```{r ancombc2_render, results='asis'}
## DT widgets do not auto-print under results='asis', so build the whole ANCOM-BC2
## tabset as Rmd source (headers + kc_block tables) and knit_child it.
.ancombc2_dt <- function(df, cap, csv) {
  if (is.null(df) || !is.data.frame(df) || nrow(df) == 0)
    return(paste0("_", cap, " -- no results at this rank._\n"))
  write.csv(df, file.path(.report_dir, csv), row.names = FALSE)
  kc_block(fgcz_dt(df, cap))
}
.ancombc2_lines <- character(0)
for (rk in c("Species", "Genus", "Family")) {
  if (rk == "Species" && !has_species) next
  res <- .ancombc2_res[[rk]]
  .ancombc2_lines <- c(.ancombc2_lines, sprintf("### %s {.tabset}", rk), "")
  if (is.null(res)) {
    msg <- .ancombc2_msg[[rk]]
    .ancombc2_lines <- c(.ancombc2_lines,
      paste0("_ANCOM-BC2 could not be computed at ", rk,
             if (!is.null(msg)) paste0(": ", msg) else " (insufficient data or ANCOMBC unavailable)", "._"), "")
    next
  }
  if (isTRUE(attr(res, "sz_fallback")))
    .ancombc2_lines <- c(.ancombc2_lines,
      paste0("_Note: all taxa were flagged as structural zeros at ", rk,
             ", so results below were recomputed with `struc_zero = FALSE`._"), "")
  lrk <- tolower(rk)
  .ancombc2_lines <- c(.ancombc2_lines, "#### Primary", "",
    .ancombc2_dt(res$res, paste0("ANCOM-BC2 -- primary results (", rk, ")"),
                 paste0("ancombc2_", lrk, "_primary.csv")))
  if (length(group_levels) > 2) {
    .ancombc2_lines <- c(.ancombc2_lines, "#### Global", "",
      .ancombc2_dt(res$res_global, paste0("ANCOM-BC2 -- global test (", rk, ")"),
                   paste0("ancombc2_", lrk, "_global.csv")))
    .ancombc2_lines <- c(.ancombc2_lines, "#### Pairwise", "",
      .ancombc2_dt(res$res_pair, paste0("ANCOM-BC2 -- pairwise contrasts (", rk, ")"),
                   paste0("ancombc2_", lrk, "_pairwise.csv")))
    .ancombc2_lines <- c(.ancombc2_lines, "#### Dunnett", "",
      .ancombc2_dt(res$res_dunn, paste0("ANCOM-BC2 -- Dunnett vs reference (", rk, ")"),
                   paste0("ancombc2_", lrk, "_dunnett.csv")))
  } else {
    .ancombc2_lines <- c(.ancombc2_lines, "#### Contrast note", "",
      "_Only two groups present: the primary contrast already covers the single pairwise comparison; the Global test and Dunnett contrasts are not applicable._", "")
  }
}
kc_render(.ancombc2_lines)
```

## DESeq2 + edgeR {.tabset}

### DESeq2 (per-contrast)

```{r deseq2_run}
.deseq_reses <- NULL
.sigtab      <- NULL
tryCatch({
  Group <- get_variable(physeq_filtered, grouping)
  ds2   <- phyloseq_to_deseq2(physeq_filtered, ~ 0 + Group)
  geoMeans <- apply(counts(ds2), 1, gm_mean)
  ds2 <- estimateSizeFactors(ds2, geoMeans = geoMeans)
  ds2 <- DESeq(ds2, test = "Wald", fitType = "local")

  if (length(group_levels) >= 2) {
    comps <- combn(group_levels, 2, simplify = FALSE)
    reses <- do.call(rbind, lapply(comps, function(i) {
      a <- as.character(i[1]); b <- as.character(i[2])
      r <- as.data.frame(results(ds2, contrast = c(grouping, a, b), cooksCutoff = FALSE))
      r$log10p   <- -log10(r$pvalue)
      r$contrast <- paste0(a, "-", b)
      cbind(r, as(tax_table(physeq_filtered)[rownames(r), ], "matrix"))
    }))
    .deseq_reses <- reses[!duplicated(reses), ]
    .sigtab      <- .deseq_reses[which(.deseq_reses$padj < 0.01), ]
    write.csv(.deseq_reses, file.path(.report_dir, "deseq2_all.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  }
}, error = function(e) cat("DESeq2 failed: ", e$message, "\n"))
```

```{r deseq2_plots, fig.width=12, fig.height=6}
if (!is.null(.deseq_reses)) {
  p2 <- ggplot(.deseq_reses, aes(x = log2FoldChange)) + geom_histogram(bins = 60) +
          facet_wrap(~contrast, ncol = 3) + theme_bw()
  p3 <- ggplot(.deseq_reses, aes(x = pvalue)) + geom_histogram(bins = 60) +
          facet_wrap(~contrast, ncol = 3) + theme_bw()
  print(p2 / p3)
}
```

### edgeR (per-contrast)

```{r edger_run}
.edger_all <- NULL
.edger_sig <- NULL
tryCatch({
  Group <- get_variable(physeq_filtered, grouping)
  design <- model.matrix(~ 0 + Group)
  dge <- as(otu_table(physeq_filtered), "matrix") + 1L
  taxonomy <- data.frame(as.data.frame(tax_table(physeq_filtered)), "matrix")
  dge <- DGEList(counts = dge, group = Group, genes = taxonomy, remove.zeros = TRUE)
  dge <- calcNormFactors(dge, method = "RLE")
  dge <- estimateGLMCommonDisp(dge, design)
  dge <- estimateGLMTrendedDisp(dge, design)
  dge <- estimateGLMTagwiseDisp(dge, design)
  fit <- glmFit(dge, design)
  comps <- combn(group_levels, 2, simplify = FALSE)
  resAll <- do.call(rbind, lapply(comps, function(i) {
    myargs <- list(paste0("Group", i[1], "-Group", i[2]), levels = design)
    my.con <- do.call(makeContrasts, myargs)
    lrt <- glmLRT(fit, contrast = my.con)
    res <- topTags(lrt, n = nrow(dge), adjust.method = "BH", sort.by = "PValue")@.Data[[1]]
    res <- tibble::rownames_to_column(res, "ASV")
    res$log10p   <- -log10(res$PValue)
    res$contrast <- paste0(i[1], "-", i[2])
    res
  }))
  .edger_all <- resAll[!duplicated(resAll), ]
  .edger_sig <- .edger_all[.edger_all$FDR <= 0.01, ]
  write.csv(.edger_all, file.path(.report_dir, "edger_all.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
}, error = function(e) cat("edgeR failed: ", e$message, "\n"))
```

```{r edger_plots, fig.width=12, fig.height=6}
if (!is.null(.edger_all)) {
  p2 <- ggplot(.edger_all, aes(x = logFC)) + geom_histogram(bins = 60) +
          facet_wrap(~contrast, ncol = 3) + theme_bw()
  p3 <- ggplot(.edger_all, aes(x = PValue)) + geom_histogram(bins = 60) +
          facet_wrap(~contrast, ncol = 3) + theme_bw()
  print(p2 / p3)
}
```

### Combined DESeq2 + edgeR

```{r combined_venn, fig.width=12, fig.height=6}
if (!is.null(.sigtab) && !is.null(.edger_sig)) {
  contrasts <- intersect(unique(.sigtab$contrast), unique(.edger_sig$contrast))
  if (length(contrasts) > 0) {
    ggs <- lapply(contrasts, function(crb) {
      ggvenn(list(DESeq2 = rownames(.sigtab[.sigtab$contrast == crb, ]),
                  edgeR  = .edger_sig$ASV[.edger_sig$contrast == crb]),
             text_size = 4) + ggtitle(crb)
    })
    print(ggarrange(plotlist = ggs, ncol = min(length(ggs), 3)))
  }

  ## Overlap table
  res_overlap <- do.call(rbind, lapply(contrasts, function(crb) {
    a <- rownames(.sigtab[.sigtab$contrast == crb, ])
    b <- .edger_sig$ASV[.edger_sig$contrast == crb]
    ov <- intersect(a, b)
    if (length(ov) == 0) return(NULL)
    data.frame(contrast = crb, ASV = ov, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  }))
  if (!is.null(res_overlap)) {
    write.csv(res_overlap, file.path(.report_dir, "deseq2_edger_overlap.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
    fgcz_dt(res_overlap, "ASVs significant in both DESeq2 and edgeR")
  }
} else {
  cat("Not enough significant results to build the overlap.\n")
}
```

## LEfSe {.tabset}

LEfSe (Linear discriminant analysis Effect Size, via `microbiomeMarker::run_lefse`) combines a
Kruskal-Wallis test across groups with an LDA effect-size estimate to find taxa that best
characterise each group. Run at Species (when available), Genus and Family.

```{r lefse_helpers}
.lefse_msg <- list()
.run_lefse_rank <- function(rank) {
  if (!.have_microbiomeMrk || is.null(physeq_filtered) || length(group_levels) < 2) return(NULL)
  if (rank == "Species" && !has_species) return(NULL)
  tryCatch(
    microbiomeMarker::run_lefse(physeq_filtered, group = "Group", taxa_rank = rank,
                                norm = "CPM", kw_cutoff = 0.05,
                                wilcoxon_cutoff = 0.05, lda_cutoff = 2),
    error = function(e) { .lefse_msg[[rank]] <<- e$message; NULL })
}
```

```{r lefse_render, results='asis'}
.lefse_lines <- character(0)
for (rk in c("Species", "Genus", "Family")) {
  if (rk == "Species" && !has_species) next
  mm  <- .run_lefse_rank(rk)
  ## marker_table is an S4 class that *contains* data.frame -- coerce via as(.,"data.frame").
  mt  <- if (!is.null(mm)) tryCatch(as(microbiomeMarker::marker_table(mm), "data.frame"),
                                    error = function(e) NULL) else NULL
  if (is.null(mt) || nrow(mt) == 0) {
    msg <- .lefse_msg[[rk]]
    .lefse_lines <- c(.lefse_lines, sprintf("### %s", rk), "",
      paste0("_No significant LEfSe markers at ", rk,
             if (!is.null(msg)) paste0(" (", msg, ")") else "", "._"), "")
    next
  }
  write.csv(mt, file.path(.report_dir, paste0("lefse_", tolower(rk), ".csv")), row.names = FALSE)
  p <- tryCatch(microbiomeMarker::plot_ef_bar(mm), error = function(e) NULL)
  .lefse_lines <- c(.lefse_lines, sprintf("### %s {.tabset}", rk), "")
  if (!is.null(p))
    .lefse_lines <- c(.lefse_lines, "#### Effect size (LDA)", "",
                      kc_block(p, chunk_opts = "fig.width=10, fig.height=7"))
  .lefse_lines <- c(.lefse_lines, "#### Marker table", "",
                    kc_block(fgcz_dt(mt, paste0("LEfSe markers (", rk, ")"))))
}
if (!.have_microbiomeMrk)
  .lefse_lines <- "_microbiomeMarker package not available -- LEfSe skipped._"
kc_render(.lefse_lines)
```

## ALDEx2 (taxonomic) {.tabset}

ALDEx2 on the taxonomic count tables (in addition to its use on the PICRUSt2 functional
profiles). For more than two groups it uses the Kruskal-Wallis / GLM test; for two groups the
Welch/Wilcoxon test with effect sizes. Run at Species (when available), Genus and Family.

```{r aldex_taxa_helpers}
.da_rank_ps      <- list(Species = physeq_Species, Genus = physeq_Genus, Family = physeq_Family)
.aldex_taxa_msg  <- list()

## Readable per-taxon labels for an agglomerated phyloseq at `rank`.
.rank_labels <- function(ps, rank) {
  tax <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ps), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  lab <- as.character(tax[[rank]])
  blank <- is.na(lab) | !nzchar(lab)
  lab[blank] <- rownames(tax)[blank]
  make.unique(lab)
}

.aldex_taxa_df <- function(rank) {
  ps <- .da_rank_ps[[rank]]
  if (!.have_aldex2 || is.null(ps) || ntaxa(ps) < 2 || length(group_levels) < 2) return(NULL)
  otu <- as(otu_table(ps), "matrix"); if (!taxa_are_rows(ps)) otu <- t(otu)
  groups <- as.character(sample_data(ps)$Group)
  test <- if (length(unique(groups)) > 2) "kw" else "t"
  res <- tryCatch(
    ALDEx2::aldex(round(otu), conditions = groups, mc.samples = 128,
                  test = test, effect = (test == "t"), denom = "all", verbose = FALSE),
    error = function(e) { .aldex_taxa_msg[[rank]] <<- e$message; NULL })
  if (is.null(res)) return(NULL)
  res <- as.data.frame(res)
  lab <- .rank_labels(ps, rank)
  res$Taxon <- lab[match(rownames(res), rownames(otu))]
  res[, c("Taxon", setdiff(colnames(res), "Taxon")), drop = FALSE]
}

## Bar chart of the top features by ALDEx2 significance (works for kw and t tests).
.aldex_taxa_plot <- function(df) {
  pcol <- intersect(c("kw.eBH", "we.eBH", "wi.eBH", "glm.eBH"), colnames(df))
  if (length(pcol) == 0) return(NULL)
  pcol <- pcol[1]
  d <- df[order(df[[pcol]]), , drop = FALSE]
  d <- head(d, 20)
  d$score <- -log10(pmax(d[[pcol]], .Machine$double.xmin))
  d$Taxon <- factor(d$Taxon, levels = rev(unique(d$Taxon)))
  ggplot(d, aes(x = Taxon, y = score)) +
    geom_col(fill = "steelblue") + coord_flip() +
    labs(x = NULL, y = paste0("-log10(", pcol, ")"),
         title = "Top 20 features by ALDEx2 significance") + theme_bw()
}
```

```{r aldex_taxa_render, results='asis'}
.aldex_taxa_lines <- character(0)
for (rk in c("Species", "Genus", "Family")) {
  if (rk == "Species" && !has_species) next
  df <- .aldex_taxa_df(rk)
  if (is.null(df) || nrow(df) == 0) {
    msg <- .aldex_taxa_msg[[rk]]
    .aldex_taxa_lines <- c(.aldex_taxa_lines, sprintf("### %s", rk), "",
      paste0("_ALDEx2 produced no result at ", rk,
             if (!is.null(msg)) paste0(" (", msg, ")") else "", "._"), "")
    next
  }
  write.csv(df, file.path(.report_dir, paste0("aldex2_taxa_", tolower(rk), ".csv")), row.names = FALSE)
  .aldex_taxa_lines <- c(.aldex_taxa_lines, sprintf("### %s {.tabset}", rk), "")
  p <- .aldex_taxa_plot(df)
  if (!is.null(p))
    .aldex_taxa_lines <- c(.aldex_taxa_lines, "#### Significance plot", "",
                           kc_block(p, chunk_opts = "fig.width=10, fig.height=7"))
  .aldex_taxa_lines <- c(.aldex_taxa_lines, "#### Table", "",
                         kc_block(fgcz_dt(df, paste0("ALDEx2 (", rk, ")"))))
}
if (!.have_aldex2)
  .aldex_taxa_lines <- "_ALDEx2 package not available -- taxonomic ALDEx2 skipped._"
kc_render(.aldex_taxa_lines)
```

# PICRUSt2 {.tabset}

```{r picrust_gate}
.picrust_enabled <- isTRUE(.cfg$run_picrust2) &&
                    ((exists("ko_abundance") && !is.null(ko_abundance)) ||
                     (exists("ec_abundance") && !is.null(ec_abundance)) ||
                     (exists("pathway_abundance") && !is.null(pathway_abundance)) ||
                     (exists("picrust2_ko") && !is.null(picrust2_ko)) ||
                     (exists("picrust2_ec") && !is.null(picrust2_ec)) ||
                     (exists("picrust2_pathway") && !is.null(picrust2_pathway)))
if (!.picrust_enabled) {
  cat("PICRUSt2 not requested or input objects not present -- section skipped.\n")
}

## Normalise input names to picrust2_ko / picrust2_ec / picrust2_pathway.
## Parentheses required so R sees the whole if/else-if/else as one expression.
.pi_ko   <- (if (exists("picrust2_ko")      && !is.null(picrust2_ko))      picrust2_ko
             else if (exists("ko_abundance") && !is.null(ko_abundance))    ko_abundance else NULL)
.pi_ec   <- (if (exists("picrust2_ec")      && !is.null(picrust2_ec))      picrust2_ec
             else if (exists("ec_abundance") && !is.null(ec_abundance))    ec_abundance else NULL)
.pi_path <- (if (exists("picrust2_pathway") && !is.null(picrust2_pathway)) picrust2_pathway
             else if (exists("pathway_abundance") && !is.null(pathway_abundance)) pathway_abundance else NULL)
```

```{r picrust_helpers}
.daa_aldex2 <- function(mat, groups) {
  if (!.have_aldex2) return(NULL)
  tryCatch({
    aldex_res <- ALDEx2::aldex(round(as.matrix(mat)), conditions = groups,
                               mc.samples = 64, test = "kw", effect = TRUE,
                               include.sample.summary = FALSE, denom = "all", verbose = FALSE)
    aldex_res <- tibble::rownames_to_column(as.data.frame(aldex_res), "feature")
    aldex_res
  }, error = function(e) { cat("ALDEx2 failed: ", e$message, "\n"); NULL })
}

.daa_metagenomeSeq <- function(mat, groups) {
  if (!.have_metagenomeSeq) return(NULL)
  tryCatch({
    pairs <- combn(unique(groups), 2, simplify = FALSE)
    results_list <- lapply(pairs, function(p) {
      keep <- groups %in% p
      m2   <- mat[, keep, drop = FALSE]
      g2   <- groups[keep]
      m2   <- m2[rowSums(m2) > 0, , drop = FALSE]
      if (ncol(m2) < 4) return(NULL)
      ## AnnotatedDataFrame / pData are exported by Biobase, not metagenomeSeq.
      pheno <- Biobase::AnnotatedDataFrame(data.frame(Group = g2,
                                                      row.names = colnames(m2)))
      mr <- metagenomeSeq::newMRexperiment(round(m2), phenoData = pheno)
      mr <- metagenomeSeq::cumNorm(mr, p = metagenomeSeq::cumNormStat(mr))
      design <- model.matrix(~ Group, data = Biobase::pData(mr))
      fit    <- metagenomeSeq::fitFeatureModel(mr, design)
      res    <- metagenomeSeq::MRcoefs(fit, number = nrow(m2))
      res$feature  <- rownames(res)
      res$contrast <- paste(p[2], "vs", p[1])
      res
    })
    do.call(rbind, results_list[!sapply(results_list, is.null)])
  }, error = function(e) { cat("metagenomeSeq failed: ", e$message, "\n"); NULL })
}

## ---- Functional abundance heatmap (top-N features) ----
## Per-sample CLR (features x samples): log(x+1) minus the per-sample mean-log.
.picrust_clr <- function(m) {
  m <- m + 1
  sweep(log(m), 2, colMeans(log(m)), "-")
}
## Clustered-ish abundance heatmap: pick the top-N features by total abundance,
## then colour by per-feature Z-score (RAW) or per-sample CLR, faceted by group.
.picrust_heatmap <- function(mat, groups, topN, mode = c("raw", "clr"), label = "") {
  mode <- match.arg(mode)
  if (nrow(mat) < 2 || ncol(mat) < 2) return(NULL)
  keep <- head(order(rowSums(mat, na.rm = TRUE), decreasing = TRUE), min(topN, nrow(mat)))
  m <- mat[keep, , drop = FALSE]
  if (mode == "clr") {
    m <- .picrust_clr(m); fillname <- "CLR"
  } else {
    m <- t(scale(t(m))); m[is.na(m)] <- 0; fillname <- "Z-score"
  }
  ord <- rownames(m)                              # keep abundance ordering
  df  <- reshape2::melt(as.matrix(m), varnames = c("feature", "SampleID"), value.name = "val")
  df$feature  <- factor(df$feature, levels = rev(ord))
  df$Group    <- groups[match(as.character(df$SampleID), colnames(mat))]
  ggplot(df, aes(x = SampleID, y = feature, fill = val)) +
    geom_tile() +
    facet_grid(. ~ Group, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") +
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "navy", mid = "white", high = "firebrick",
                         midpoint = 0, name = fillname) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = NULL,
         title = paste0(label, " -- top ", nrow(m), " features (", toupper(mode), ")")) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, size = 6),
          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 6),
          panel.grid  = element_blank())
}

## Top-feature significance bar chart from an ALDEx2 result frame.
.picrust_aldex_bar <- function(ald, label) {
  pcol <- intersect(c("kw.eBH", "we.eBH", "wi.eBH", "glm.eBH"), colnames(ald))
  if (length(pcol) == 0) return(NULL)
  pcol <- pcol[1]
  d <- head(ald[order(ald[[pcol]]), , drop = FALSE], 20)
  if (nrow(d) == 0) return(NULL)
  d$score <- -log10(pmax(d[[pcol]], .Machine$double.xmin))
  d$feature <- factor(d$feature, levels = rev(unique(d$feature)))
  ggplot(d, aes(x = feature, y = score)) +
    geom_col(fill = "steelblue") + coord_flip() +
    labs(x = NULL, y = paste0("-log10(", pcol, ")"),
         title = paste("ALDEx2 top features --", label)) + theme_bw()
}
## ALDEx2 volcano (effect vs significance); only defined for the 2-group case.
.picrust_aldex_volcano <- function(ald, label) {
  if (!("effect" %in% colnames(ald))) return(NULL)
  pcol <- intersect(c("we.eBH", "wi.eBH"), colnames(ald))
  if (length(pcol) == 0) return(NULL)
  pcol <- pcol[1]
  d <- ald
  d$sig <- d[[pcol]] < 0.05
  ggplot(d, aes(x = effect, y = -log10(pmax(d[[pcol]], .Machine$double.xmin)), color = sig)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c(`FALSE` = "grey60", `TRUE` = "firebrick"), guide = "none") +
    labs(x = "ALDEx2 effect size", y = paste0("-log10(", pcol, ")"),
         title = paste("ALDEx2 volcano --", label)) + theme_bw()
}
## metagenomeSeq per-contrast top-feature bar chart.
.picrust_mgseq_bar <- function(mg, label) {
  if (is.null(mg) || nrow(mg) == 0 || !("adjPvalues" %in% colnames(mg))) return(NULL)
  d <- mg %>%
    dplyr::group_by(contrast) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(adjPvalues, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
    dplyr::slice_head(n = 15) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup()
  if (nrow(d) == 0) return(NULL)
  d$score <- -log10(pmax(d$adjPvalues, .Machine$double.xmin))
  d$feature <- factor(d$feature, levels = rev(unique(d$feature)))
  ggplot(d, aes(x = feature, y = score)) +
    geom_col(fill = "darkorange") + coord_flip() +
    facet_wrap(~contrast, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
    labs(x = NULL, y = "-log10(adjusted p)",
         title = paste("metagenomeSeq top features --", label)) + theme_bw()
}

.render_picrust_panel <- function(mat, label) {
  if (is.null(mat)) { cat("No ", label, " input.\n"); return(invisible(NULL)) }
  ## Defensive: if the input still has a non-numeric first column (e.g. the
  ## "function" / "pathway" ID column from a naive read.table), hoist it to
  ## rownames so as.matrix() doesn't force everything to character.
  if (is.data.frame(mat) && ncol(mat) >= 2 && !is.numeric(mat[[1]])) {
    .ids <- as.character(mat[[1]])
    mat  <- mat[, -1, drop = FALSE]
    rownames(mat) <- make.unique(.ids)
  }
  mat <- as.matrix(mat)
  storage.mode(mat) <- "numeric"
  if (ncol(mat) < 4 || nrow(mat) < 2) {
    cat("Insufficient ", label, " data (", nrow(mat), " features x ", ncol(mat), " samples).\n", sep = "")
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }
  samp_ids <- intersect(colnames(mat), metadata$SampleID)
  if (length(samp_ids) < 4) {
    cat("Not enough samples shared with metadata for ", label, ".\n")
    return(invisible(NULL))
  }
  mat <- mat[, samp_ids, drop = FALSE]
  groups <- metadata$Group[match(samp_ids, metadata$SampleID)]

  ## Compute DAA first (these may cat a specific failure message), then emit all
  ## sub-sections (headers + tables + plots) through knit_child so the DT/plot
  ## widgets actually render. Tables are shown in full (DT length-menu handles rows).
  ald <- .daa_aldex2(mat, groups)
  mg  <- .daa_metagenomeSeq(mat, groups)

  ## ---- Abundance heatmaps ----
  lines <- c("### Abundance heatmaps {.tabset}", "")
  hm <- list(
    c("#### Top 15 (RAW)",  "raw", 15L), c("#### Top 30 (RAW)",  "raw", 30L),
    c("#### Top 15 (CLR)",  "clr", 15L), c("#### Top 30 (CLR)",  "clr", 30L))
  for (h in hm) {
    p <- tryCatch(.picrust_heatmap(mat, groups, as.integer(h[3]), h[2], label),
                  error = function(e) NULL)
    lines <- c(lines, h[1], "",
               if (is.null(p)) "_Heatmap unavailable for this level._\n"
               else kc_block(p, chunk_opts = "fig.width=12, fig.height=8"))
  }

  ## ---- ALDEx2 ----
  lines <- c(lines, "### ALDEx2 (global, all groups) {.tabset}", "")
  if (!is.null(ald)) {
    write.csv(ald, file.path(.report_dir, paste0("picrust2_aldex2_", label, ".csv")), row.names = FALSE)
    lines <- c(lines, "#### Table", "",
               kc_block(fgcz_dt(ald[order(ald$kw.eBH), ],
                                paste("ALDEx2 --", label, "(sorted by KW eBH p)"))))
    pbar <- .picrust_aldex_bar(ald, label)
    lines <- c(lines, "#### Top features", "",
               if (is.null(pbar)) "_No significance column for a bar chart._\n"
               else kc_block(pbar, chunk_opts = "fig.width=10, fig.height=7"))
    pvol <- .picrust_aldex_volcano(ald, label)
    lines <- c(lines, "#### Volcano", "",
               if (is.null(pvol))
                 "_Volcano (effect size) is only available for two-group comparisons; with >2 groups ALDEx2 uses the Kruskal-Wallis/GLM test, which has no single effect size._\n"
               else kc_block(pvol, chunk_opts = "fig.width=9, fig.height=7"))
  } else {
    lines <- c(lines, "_No ALDEx2 result for this level._\n")
  }

  ## ---- metagenomeSeq ----
  lines <- c(lines, "### metagenomeSeq (pairwise) {.tabset}", "")
  if (!is.null(mg) && nrow(mg) > 0) {
    write.csv(mg, file.path(.report_dir, paste0("picrust2_metagenomeSeq_", label, ".csv")), row.names = FALSE)
    lines <- c(lines, "#### Table", "",
               kc_block(fgcz_dt(mg[order(mg$adjPvalues), ],
                                paste("metagenomeSeq --", label, "(sorted by adjusted p)"))))
    pmg <- .picrust_mgseq_bar(mg, label)
    lines <- c(lines, "#### Top features", "",
               if (is.null(pmg)) "_No significance column for a bar chart._\n"
               else kc_block(pmg, chunk_opts = "fig.width=12, fig.height=8"))
  } else {
    lines <- c(lines, "_No metagenomeSeq result for this level._\n")
  }
  kc_render(lines)
}
```

## MetaCyc Pathways {.tabset}

```{r picrust_pathway, eval=.picrust_enabled, results='asis'}
.render_picrust_panel(.pi_path, "pathway")
```

## KEGG Orthologs (KO) {.tabset}

```{r picrust_ko, eval=.picrust_enabled, results='asis'}
.render_picrust_panel(.pi_ko, "ko")
```

## Enzyme Commission (EC) {.tabset}

```{r picrust_ec, eval=.picrust_enabled, results='asis'}
.render_picrust_panel(.pi_ec, "ec")
```

## ASV contributors

```{r picrust_contributors, eval=.picrust_enabled, results='asis'}
.contrib_path <- if (exists("picrust2_contrib") && !is.null(picrust2_contrib)) picrust2_contrib else NULL
if (!is.null(.contrib_path) && length(.contrib_path) > 0 && file.exists(.contrib_path[1])) {
  contrib <- tryCatch(read.delim(.contrib_path[1], sep = "\t", check.names = FALSE),
                      error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(contrib) || nrow(contrib) == 0) {
    cat("Stratified PICRUSt2 file was present but empty or unreadable -- skipping.\n\n")
  } else {
    cn <- colnames(contrib)
    top <- NULL
    if (all(c("function", "taxon", "taxon_function_abun") %in% cn)) {
      ## Long "contrib" format (default --stratified output).
      top <- contrib %>%
        dplyr::group_by(`function`, taxon) %>%
        dplyr::summarise(mean_taxon_function_abun = mean(taxon_function_abun, na.rm = TRUE),
                         .groups = "drop") %>%
        dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(mean_taxon_function_abun))
    } else {
      ## Wide "strat" format (--stratified --wide_table): function + sequence
      ## id columns, then one numeric column per sample. Sum across samples.
      id_cols   <- intersect(c("function", "sequence", "taxon", "pathway", "OTU_ID"), cn)
      samp_cols <- setdiff(cn, id_cols)
      samp_cols <- samp_cols[sapply(contrib[samp_cols], is.numeric)]
      if (length(id_cols) >= 1 && length(samp_cols) >= 1) {
        contrib$total_abun <- rowSums(contrib[, samp_cols, drop = FALSE], na.rm = TRUE)
        top <- contrib[, c(id_cols, "total_abun"), drop = FALSE]
        top <- top[order(-top$total_abun), , drop = FALSE]
      }
    }
    if (is.null(top) || nrow(top) == 0) {
      cat("Stratified PICRUSt2 file present but its columns were not recognised ",
          "(saw: ", paste(head(cn, 10), collapse = ", "), "). Skipping.\n\n", sep = "")
    } else {
      write.csv(top, file.path(.report_dir, "picrust2_top_contributors.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
      cat("All (function, ASV) contributors by abundance (use the length menu to page through):\n\n")
      kc_render(kc_block(fgcz_dt(top, "ASV-to-function contributors")))
    }
  }
} else {
  cat("No stratified PICRUSt2 contribution file provided -- skipping.\n\n")
}
```

# Taxon-level CLR analysis {.tabset}

CLR (centered log-ratio) transforms each sample's count vector into log-ratios relative to
the geometric mean -- the natural representation for compositional sequencing data.
Per-taxon Wilcoxon tests are run on the CLR values; violin plots show the top 5 by
mean(|CLR|).

```{r clr_helpers}
clr_wilcoxon_level <- function(ps, level) {
  if (is.null(ps) || ntaxa(ps) == 0) return(NULL)
  ps_lvl <- tryCatch(tax_glom(ps, taxrank = level, NArm = TRUE), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(ps_lvl) || ntaxa(ps_lvl) == 0) return(NULL)
  mat <- as(otu_table(ps_lvl), "matrix")
  if (!taxa_are_rows(ps_lvl)) mat <- t(mat)
  tax_df <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ps_lvl), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  taxon_label <- tax_df[[level]]
  if (any(is.na(taxon_label) | taxon_label == "")) {
    bad <- which(is.na(taxon_label) | taxon_label == "")
    taxon_label[bad] <- paste0("unknown_", bad)
  }
  taxon_label <- make.unique(taxon_label)
  rownames(mat) <- taxon_label
  clr_mat <- clr_transform_matrix(mat)
  samp_groups <- sample_data(ps_lvl)$Group
  samp_groups <- as.character(samp_groups)
  group_pairs <- combn(unique(samp_groups), 2, simplify = FALSE)
  results <- do.call(rbind, lapply(rownames(clr_mat), function(tx) {
    do.call(rbind, lapply(group_pairs, function(p) {
      x <- clr_mat[tx, samp_groups == p[1]]
      y <- clr_mat[tx, samp_groups == p[2]]
      if (length(x) < 2 || length(y) < 2) return(NULL)
      w <- suppressWarnings(wilcox.test(x, y))
      data.frame(taxon = tx, group1 = p[1], group2 = p[2],
                 mean_clr_g1 = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE),
                 mean_clr_g2 = mean(y, na.rm = TRUE),
                 W = w$statistic, p = w$p.value,
                 stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    }))
  }))
  if (is.null(results) || nrow(results) == 0) return(NULL)
  results$p.adj <- p.adjust(results$p, method = "BH")
  results <- results[order(results$p.adj), ]
  attr(results, "clr_matrix") <- clr_mat
  results
}

clr_top5_violin <- function(clr_mat, samp_groups, level_name, top_n = 5) {
  if (is.null(clr_mat)) return(NULL)
  mean_abs <- rowMeans(abs(clr_mat), na.rm = TRUE)
  top <- names(sort(mean_abs, decreasing = TRUE)[seq_len(min(top_n, length(mean_abs)))])
  long <- reshape2::melt(clr_mat[top, , drop = FALSE], varnames = c("Taxon", "Sample"),
                         value.name = "CLR")
  long$Group <- samp_groups[long$Sample]
  ggplot(long, aes(x = Group, y = CLR, fill = Group)) +
    geom_violin(scale = "width") +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.1, height = 0, size = 0.7, alpha = 0.6) +
    facet_wrap(~Taxon, scales = "free_y") +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), legend.position = "none") +
    labs(title = paste("Top", length(top), level_name, "by mean(|CLR|)"))
}

render_clr_section <- function(ps, ps_label) {
  if (is.null(ps)) return(invisible(NULL))
  samp_groups <- setNames(as.character(sample_data(ps)$Group), sample_names(ps))
  ## Build the whole section as Rmd source and knit_child it, so the Pairwise
  ## Wilcoxon DT (an htmlwidget) actually renders. A print(fgcz_dt(...)) inside
  ## a results='asis' chunk emits nothing.
  lines <- character(0)
  for (rank in c("Species", "Genus", "Family", "Phylum")) {
    if (rank == "Species" && !has_species) next
    lines <- c(lines, sprintf("\n\n## CLR Wilcoxon -- %s (%s) {.tabset}\n", rank, ps_label))
    res <- clr_wilcoxon_level(ps, rank)
    if (is.null(res) || nrow(res) == 0) {
      lines <- c(lines, "_No CLR Wilcoxon results at this rank._\n")
      next
    }
    write.csv(res, file.path(.report_dir, paste0("CLR_Wilcoxon_", rank, "_", ps_label, ".csv")),
              row.names = FALSE)
    lines <- c(lines, "\n### Pairwise Wilcoxon\n",
               kc_block(fgcz_dt(head(res, 200),
                                paste(rank, "-- CLR Wilcoxon (", ps_label, ", top 200 by BH p)"))))
    p <- clr_top5_violin(attr(res, "clr_matrix"), samp_groups, rank, top_n = 5)
    lines <- c(lines, "\n### Top 5 by mean(|CLR|)\n")
    if (!is.null(p)) {
      lines <- c(lines, kc_block(p, chunk_opts = "fig.width=14, fig.height=8"))
    } else {
      lines <- c(lines, "_Not enough taxa for a violin plot._\n")
    }
  }
  kc_render(lines)
}
```

```{r clr_pre, fig.width=14, fig.height=8, results='asis'}
## No `## Pre-filter CLR` wrapper here: the outer `# Taxon-level CLR analysis
## {.tabset}` treats every level-2 header as a tab, and render_clr_section
## emits `## CLR Wilcoxon -- Rank (pre/post) {.tabset}` directly — those are
## the real tabs. A `## Pre-filter CLR` wrapper would appear as an empty
## sibling tab because content moves under its own emitted h2s.
render_clr_section(physeq, "pre")
```

```{r clr_post, fig.width=14, fig.height=8, results='asis'}
render_clr_section(physeq_filtered, "post")
```

# CLR composition barplots {.tabset}

```{r clr_barplots, fig.width=20, fig.height=10, results='asis'}
clr_barplot <- function(ps, level, label) {
  ps_lvl <- tryCatch(tax_glom(ps, level, NArm = TRUE), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(ps_lvl) || ntaxa(ps_lvl) == 0) return(NULL)
  mat <- as(otu_table(ps_lvl), "matrix")
  if (!taxa_are_rows(ps_lvl)) mat <- t(mat)
  rownames(mat) <- make.unique(as.character(tax_table(ps_lvl)[, level]))
  clr_mat <- clr_transform_matrix(mat)
  long <- reshape2::melt(clr_mat, varnames = c("Taxon", "Sample"), value.name = "CLR")
  long$Group <- as.character(sample_data(ps_lvl)$Group[match(long$Sample, sample_names(ps_lvl))])
  ggplot(long, aes(x = Sample, y = CLR, fill = Taxon)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
    facet_grid(~Group, scales = "free_x") +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, size = 7), legend.position = "none") +
    labs(title = paste("CLR composition --", level, "(", label, ")"),
         y = "CLR contribution")
}

ranks_clr_bar <- c(if (has_species) "Species" else NULL, "Genus", "Family", "Phylum")
for (lvl in ranks_clr_bar) {
  cat(sprintf("\n\n## %s {.tabset}\n\n", lvl))
  cat("\n### Pre-filter\n\n")
  p_pre <- clr_barplot(physeq, lvl, "pre")
  if (!is.null(p_pre)) print(p_pre)
  cat("\n\n### Post-filter\n\n")
  p_post <- clr_barplot(physeq_filtered, lvl, "post")
  if (!is.null(p_post)) print(p_post)
  cat("\n\n")
}
```

# CLR abundance tables export {.tabset}

```{r clr_export, results='asis'}
## Returns the DT widget (or NULL); emission is done via knit_child below so
## the widget actually renders inside the tabset.
render_clr_table <- function(ps, level, label) {
  if (is.null(ps)) return(NULL)
  ps_lvl <- tryCatch(tax_glom(ps, level, NArm = TRUE), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(ps_lvl) || ntaxa(ps_lvl) == 0) return(NULL)
  mat <- as(otu_table(ps_lvl), "matrix")
  if (!taxa_are_rows(ps_lvl)) mat <- t(mat)
  rownames(mat) <- make.unique(as.character(tax_table(ps_lvl)[, level]))
  clr_mat <- clr_transform_matrix(mat)
  raw_path <- file.path(.report_dir, paste0("raw_abundance_table_", tolower(level), "_", label, ".csv"))
  clr_path <- file.path(.report_dir, paste0("CLR_abundance_table_", tolower(level), "_", label, ".csv"))
  write.csv(as.data.frame(mat), raw_path)
  write.csv(as.data.frame(clr_mat), clr_path)
  clr_df <- data.frame(Taxon = rownames(clr_mat),
                       round(clr_mat, 3),
                       check.names = FALSE,
                       stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  fgcz_dt(clr_df, sprintf("CLR abundance -- %s (%s)", level, label))
}

ranks_clr <- c(if (has_species) "Species" else NULL, "Genus", "Family", "Phylum")
.clr_export_lines <- character(0)
for (lvl in ranks_clr) {
  .clr_export_lines <- c(.clr_export_lines, sprintf("\n\n## %s {.tabset}\n", lvl))
  dt_pre <- render_clr_table(physeq, lvl, "pre")
  .clr_export_lines <- c(.clr_export_lines, "\n### Pre-filter\n",
                         if (!is.null(dt_pre)) kc_block(dt_pre) else "_No taxa at this rank._\n")
  dt_post <- render_clr_table(physeq_filtered, lvl, "post")
  .clr_export_lines <- c(.clr_export_lines, "\n### Post-filter\n",
                         if (!is.null(dt_post)) kc_block(dt_post) else "_No taxa at this rank._\n")
}
kc_render(.clr_export_lines)
```

# General taxonomic composition barplots {.tabset}

Plots include reads whose label is missing at the current rank by relabelling them with
their parent rank (e.g. `unknown Family (Phylum=Bacteroidota)`). Useful for cross-checking
against the main barplot section, which drops `NA` rows.

```{r general_barplots, fig.width=20, fig.height=12, results='asis'}
relabel_for_general <- function(ps, rank) {
  tax <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ps), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  parent_ranks <- switch(rank,
    "Species" = c("Genus", "Family", "Order", "Class", "Phylum", "Kingdom"),
    "Genus"   = c("Family", "Order", "Class", "Phylum", "Kingdom"),
    "Family"  = c("Order", "Class", "Phylum", "Kingdom"),
    "Phylum"  = c("Kingdom"),
    character(0))
  lab <- tax[[rank]]
  bad <- is.na(lab) | lab == ""
  if (any(bad) && length(parent_ranks) > 0) {
    for (pr in parent_ranks) {
      if (pr %in% colnames(tax)) {
        lab[bad] <- paste0("unknown ", rank, " (", pr, "=", tax[bad, pr], ")")
        bad <- is.na(lab) | lab == "" | grepl("=NA", lab)
        if (!any(bad)) break
      }
    }
  }
  tax[[rank]] <- lab
  tax_table(ps) <- tax_table(as.matrix(tax))
  ps
}

## One tabset per rank with Top 20 / Top 50 as sub-tabs. Emitting two
## sibling `## Rank (Top 20)` / `## Rank (Top 50)` tabsets in a row without
## proper blank-line separation caused the next iteration's `### Plot` to
## land as a second sub-tab of the previous rank's Top 50 tabset — hence
## "Genus top 20 showing up under Family (Top 50)".
ranks_gen <- c(if (has_species) "Species" else NULL, "Genus", "Family", "Phylum")
for (rank in ranks_gen) {
  ps_lab <- relabel_for_general(physeq_filtered, rank)
  cat(sprintf("\n\n## %s {.tabset}\n\n", rank))
  cat("\n### Top 20\n\n")
  print(plot_taxa_bar_topN(ps_lab, rank, 20, paste("General Top 20", rank)))
  cat("\n\n### Top 50\n\n")
  print(plot_taxa_bar_topN(ps_lab, rank, 50, paste("General Top 50", rank)))
  cat("\n\n")
}
```

# Interactive widgets

The following widgets were saved next to this report; download or click to open in a browser:

```{r widgets_summary, results='asis'}
widget_files <- list.files(.report_dir, pattern = "^interactive_.*\\.html$", full.names = FALSE)
if (length(widget_files) > 0) {
  for (wf in sort(widget_files)) cat(sprintf("- [%s](%s)\n", wf, wf))
} else {
  cat("_No interactive widgets were generated._\n")
}
```

# Session info

<details>
<summary>Click to expand session info</summary>
```{r session_info}
sessionInfo()
```
</details>
